R., -a distance of 3284 miles and from Columbus, Ky., to Purdy, Tenn., 1224 miles,-leaving a gap of 23 miles, which will be completed by the 1st March. Magnolia Lodge, No. 259-F. and A. Masons.-Holds its regular communications every Wednesday of each month, at 7 p. M., in third story of Temperance Hall, northeast corner of St. Michael and St. Joseph streets.
Bienville Lodge, No. 348. Meets every Tuesday of each month, at 7 o'clock P. M., at Athelstan Temple, cor Dauphin and St. Joseph streets.

Aetlstan Lodge, No. 369-F. and A. Masons.-Holds its regular communications on the second Thursday of each month, at 7 o'clock P. M., in the third story of their Temple, northeast corner of Dauphin and St. Joseph streets.
Bowen Lodge, No. 241-F. and A. Masons. Located at Whistler, meets on the first Thursday of each month.

organization_name: Grand Lodge of Alabama, K. of H.
line: Grand Lodge of Alabama, K. of H.-Meets in Montgomery, on the 3d Tuesday in July, 1878. The officers of the Grand Lodge, are as follows: W. S. Greene, P. G. D., Opelika; N. H. Holmes, G. D., Montgomery; B. F. Ludwig, G. V. D., Huntsville; W. A. Shields, G. A. D., Mobile; J. R. Hoffman, G. C., Athens; J. B. Stanley, G. G., Greenville; T. G. Foster, G. R., Montgomery; J. T. Pierce, G. T., Warrior; J. M. Oliver, G. Guar., Dadeville; R. H. Briggs, G. S. Whistler; J. W. McNamee, of Opelika, Hon. C. M. Shelley, of Selma, and J. L. Smith, of Athens, G. Trustees.

organization_name: Mutual Lodge No. 501, K. of H.
line: Mutual Lodge No. 501, K. of H.-Meets in their Hall, on St. Michael street, between Royal and Water, every Friday evening.

organization_name: SONS OF TEMPERANCE, Star of Hope Division No. 4
line: The Grand Lodge of Alabama.-Will meet at Eufaula, Ala., on the first Monday in February, 1878. The officers of the Grand Lodge for 1878, are as follows: F. Rice, New Market, Madison County, G. M.; Richard E. Jones, Mobile, D. G. M.; Wm. A. Buck, Mobile, G. W.; W. A. Shields, Mobile, G. S.; S. W. Cayce, Mobile, G. T.; Jno. R. Tompkins, Mobile, and W. C. Hearn, Huntsville, Grand Representatives, The R. W. Grand Encampment of Alabama. Will meet on the day succeeding the commencement of the session of the Grand Lodge at Eufaula, Ala. Mount Ararat Encampment No. 1.-Meets on the second and fourth Thursdays of each month, at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest cor. Royal and Dauphin streets. Lee Encampment No. 18. Located at Whistler.-Meets on the second and fourth Thursdays in each month. Mobile Degree Lodge No. 1.-Meets on the first Thursday of each month, alternately, at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner of Royal and Dauphin streets, and the Hall of McInnis Lodge, No. 71, Spring Hill avenue. Alabama Lodge No. 1.--Meets every Tuesday night, at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner of Royal and Dauphin streets. Union Lodge No. 13.-Meets every Monday night, at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner of Royal and Dauphin streets. Teutonic Lodge No. 68. -Meets regularly every Wednesday night, at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner of Royal and Dauphin streets. McInnis Lodge No. 71. Meets regularly every Friday night, at Hall, on the south side of Spring Hill avenue, between Broad and Pine streets. Mechanics' Lodge No. 69. Located at Whistler.-Meets every Tuesday evening. Napoleonville Lodge No. 81.-Located at Napoleonville. Meets every Monday evening. Mobile Rebekah Degree Lodge No. 1.-Meets third Thursday of each month, at Odd Fellows' Hall. Naomi Lodge No. 5.-Daughters of Rebekah.-Meets second Wednesday of each month, at Hall, on the south side of Spring Hill avenue between Broad and Pine streets. I. O. G. T. Magnolia Lodge No. 75. -Meets regularly every Tuesday evening, at their Hall, southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson. Union Lodge No. 331. Meets regularly every Monday evening, at their Hall, 78 Dauphin street, third story. SONS OF TEMPERANCE, Star of Hope Division No. 4.-Sons of Temperance. Meets regularly every Friday evening, at Hall, southeast corner of Dauphin and Jackson streets. ORPHAN ASYLUMS. Catholic Female Orphan Asylum.-South-east corner of Conti and Franklin streets, under the immediate control of the Sisters of Charity. Sister Mary Cornelia, Superior. Catholic Male Orphan Asylum.-West side of Lafayette street, north of Dauphin way, under the immediate control of the Brothers of the Sacred Hearts. Protestant Orphan Asylum. -South side of Dauphin way, west of Broad, under the control of the Protestant Orphan Asylum Society. Church Home.- Episcopal Orphan Asylum, for Girls-Warren bet. Monroe and Eslava. For Boys-ws Wilkinson head of Palmetto. Hospitals and Infirmaries. City Hospital. North side of St. Anthony, between Jefferson and Broad streets, under the superintendence of the Sisters of Charity; Sister Gabriella, Superintendent; E. H. Fournier, M. D., Physician. United States Marine Hospital. North side of St. Anthony, between Bayou and Jefferson streets; Henry W. Hutton, M. D., Physician. Providence Infirmary. South side of St. Anthony, between Jefferson and Broad streets, under the superintendence of the Sisters of Charity; Sister Theresa, Superintendent; Rhett Goode, M. D., Physician. German Relief Association. Meets on the first Sunday in every month, at 7 o'clock P. M., at Turner Hall: M. Kelsen, President; John Meyer, Vice President; Chas. Wassmuth, Secretary; A. Grotz, Treasurer. Annual election first Sunday in January.

Protestant Orphan Asylum Society. Having control of the Protestant Orphan Asylum, is governed by a Board of Managers. composed of four ladies of each of the following denominations: Baptist, Episcopal, Methodist and Presbyterian. Annual meetings first Tuesday in December. Mrs. Dr. Ketchum, first Directress; Mrs. Sallie D. Walker, second Directress; Mrs. Daniel Wheeler, Treasurer; Miss V, W. Dorman, Secretary; Mrs. L. L. Ruggles, Matron; Miss M. Barkuloo, Teacher.

Catholic Female Charitable Society. Meets the first Tuesday in every month, at the Female Orphan Asylum, northwest corner Conti and Claiborne streets. President, Mrs. S. Riley; 1st Vice President, Mrs. R. C. Macy; Second Vice President, Mrs. M. Caulfield; Secretary, Mrs. J. Masterson Treasurer, Mrs. C. Burns; Managers, Mrs. J. Lyons, Mrs. Caulfield, Mrs. Riley, Mrs. W. McDonnell, Mrs. W. O'Connell, Mrs. E. Colston, Mrs. P. Kilduff, Mrs. J. Kavanagh, Mrs. J. McPhillips, Miss M. A. Robb and Miss D. McGill. Annual election second Tuesday in February.
Catholic Male Charitable Society, meets same as the above and has the same officers.

Female Benevolent Society. Has charge of the "Widows' Row," on the north side of Eslava, between Warren and Dearborn streets, and extends relief to the sick and needy. Annual meeting first Monday in January.

St. Patrick's Benevolent Association. An organization formed by Irishmen and their descendants, for charitable purposes. The Society meets the second Tuesday evening in each month, at St. Patrick's Hall, southwest corner of Conti and Claiborne streets. Officers: John O'Donnell, President; D. McGinnis, Vice President; B. McGovern, Treasurer; S. J. Russell, Secretary. Annual election December 12.

St. Vincent's Literary & Benevolent Association. Organized 1871. Meets regularly every third Sunday in the month, at their Hall on Palmetto, between Hamilton and Lawrence streets. James G. Terry, President; Peter Horta, Vice President; W. Hickey, Secretary; M. Toomey, Treasurer; S. J. Doss, Librarian;-- - Assistant Librarian; Wm. V. Beroujon, Marshal; E. Martinez, Banner Bearer. Annual election third Sunday in October.

Catholic Total Abstinence Society. Meets the 1st Thursday in each month, at St. Joseph's Hall, corner of Bayou and St. Louis streets. Rev. P. Imsand, S. J., Spiritual Director; N. Phelan, President; John H. Fox, Secretary. Annual election first Thursday in January.

organization_name: St. Patrick's Reading Room & Library Society
line: St. Patrick's Reading Room & Library Society, Rev. Father Kirwan, Director and President; A. Fitzpatrick, Vice President; John McCafferty, Secretary; Philip Kearney, Treasurer. Annual election first Sunday in October.

Spanish Benevolent Society. Instituted 1870. Meets regularly on the 1st Sunday of every month. President, P. Horta; Vice President, J. Pidal; Secretary to Vice President, J. A. DeOrnellas; Secretary, B. Corrales; Treasurer, P. Lema; Collector, J. Lago. Annual election in April.

organization_name: French Mutual Aid and Benevolent Association
line: French Mutual Aid and Benevolent Association. Meets on the 1st Monday night of every month, at 12 north Joachim. A. Lambert, President; E. Carre, Vice President; C. DeVaux, Secretary; E. Fichet, Treasurer. Annual election 1st Monday in November.

Ladies' Hebrew Benevolent Society. Instituted in 1850. Quarterly meetings 1st Sunday in January, April, July and October. Room in the basement of the Jackson street Synagogue, between St. Michael and St. Louis streets. Mrs. J. Goldstuker, President; Mrs. S. Frolichstein, Vice President; Mrs. B. Levy, Secretary; Mrs. D. Forchheimer, Treasurer.

organization_name: Congregation Shangarai Shamayim and Society of the Friends of the Needy
line: Congregation Shangarai Shamayim and Society of the Friends of the Needy. A. Proskauer, President; M. Neubrik, Vice President; N. Strauss, Treasurer; A. Baerman, Secretary; Rev. Dr. A. Moses, Minister; S. Schlessinger, Choir Leader and Organist; S. Markstein, Collector.

Independent Order of Bnai Brith. Beth Zur Lodge No. 84,-Meets every Monday evening, at their Hall, southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson streets. S. H. Solomon, President; M. Goldsmith, Vice President; A. Newman, Secretary; S. L. Hahn, Treasurer. Semi-annual elections 1st week in January and July.

Lee Association. Meets the second Thursday in each month, at the hall of Mechanics' Steam Fire Company No. 7: W. T. Walthall, President; Chas. De Vaux, Secretary. Annual election the second Thursday in January.

Fidelia Society. Alfred Proskauer, President; S. Levy, Vice President; E. Richard, Secretary; B. Kahn, Treasurer; s. H. Solomon, Dramatic Director English Department; S. Schlessinger, Dramatic Director German Department and Musical Director; J. Bloch, Assistant Musical Director; S. Markstein, Messenger. Annual election first Sunday in May.

Mobile Turn Verein. Meets on the first day of every month at 7 o'clock P. M., at their Hall on St. Emanuel street. S. Vogel, President; G. Menken, Chief of Gymnasts; C. Wassmuth, Recording Secretary; John Gerst, Treasurer; John Lang, Vice President; H. Picker, Corresponding Secretary; Otto Fleming, Property Keeper. Semi-annual election January 1st and July 1st.

organization_name: Hay Makers; or Oriental Order of Humility
line: Hay Makers; or Oriental Order of Humility. Grand Sultan, T. A. Blackman; Grand Prophet, J. F. Summersell; Grand Vizer, W. H. Sheffield; Grand Master of Cavalry, H. G. Kearns; Grand Captain of the Guard, Dave Levi; Grand Minister of Finance, T. W. Williams; Grand Scribe, L. H. Kennerly; Grand Conductor, C. F. Summersell; Grand Sentinel, Geo. Winter.

Mobile Irving Association. Organized December 15, 1855. President, W. H. Sheffield; Vice President, J. F. Summersell; Secretary, H. P. Robertson; Treasurer, J. W. Clonan; Dramatic Director, H. G. Kearns; Properties, W. R. Gerow; Assistant Properties, J. P. Emerich, Jr.; Floor Manager, E. Scheuermann; Assistant Floor Manager, H. C. Bekurs.

Forrest Club. Organized 1873. Meets every Thursday evening. President, R. T. Shultz; Vice President, George Haig; Secretary and Treasurer, Jno. J. Cragin; Stage Manager, B. C. Rowan; Business Manager, A. B. Dure; Prompter, Wm. Provost.
Arcadian Dramatic Club. Organized February 22d, 1875. President, Geo. Vautrot; Stage Manager, D. R. Duval; Secretary and Treasurer, D. Williams. Meets every second Monday in the month.

Creole Social Club. Instituted 1856. Jules Mitchell, President; Jno. B. Guison, Vice President; Samuel Pollard, Secretary; F. Andry, Jr., Treasurer; S. Durette, Steward. Annual election first Sunday in October.

Can't Get Away Club. Instituted in 1839. Annual meeting first Monday in July. Officers: President, Reuben Nason; 1st Vice-President, Alexander Anderson; 2d Vice President, John D. Haynie; Secretaries, W. T. Walthall and Wm. H. Higley; Treasurer, M. F. Tucker.

Gulf City Grange No. 68. Meets the 2d Tuesday of every month, at their Hall, south side of Spring Hill ave 7 west of Broad. Master, T. W. Moore; Overseer, B. F. Rowland; Lecturer, Frank Draxler; Steward, A. G. Moore; Assistant Steward, H. F. Acker; Chaplain, M. McInnis; Treasurer, J. C. Calhoun; Secretary, J. H. Fitzpatrick; Gate Keeper, Henry Schumacher; Ceres, Mrs. A. G. Moore; Pomona, Mrs. S. A. Calhoun; Flora, Miss Amanda A. Calhoun; Assistant Lady Steward, Mrs. T. W. Moore; Musician, Miss L. M. Moore. Annual election fourth Tuesday in December.

Agricultural, Mechanical and Horticultural Association. Meets first Wednesday in each month. The Annual Fair is held the first week in May. The Association is controlled by a board of sixteen directors and the following officers: C. C. Langdon, President; Parker, 2d Vice-President; L. Brewer, 3d-, 1st Vice President; F. S. Vice President; R. Dane, 4th Vice President; Geo. F. Werborn, Treasurer; W. E. Mickle, Secretary.

Mobile Board of Trade-1877-78. President, Leslie E. Brooks; first Vice President, Leroy Brewer; second Vice President, D. McNeill; Treasurer, Ira W. R. W. Fort. Porter; Secretary and Superintendent, BOARD OF CONTROL. Daniel Wheeler, Thos. A. Hamilton, L. F. Irwin, Ira W. Porter, W. S. Foster, A. C. Danner, Jno. Maguire, M. McCarthy. C. E. Thames, Thos. Henry, Sr., Joseph Hodgson, G. B. Preston.

Mobile Cotton Exchange-1877--78. A. J. Gillespie, President; A. J. Ingersoll, Vice President; L. C. Dorgan, Treasurer; R. H. Bolling, Superintendent; Board of Directors, D. E. Huger, W. H. Gardner, James A. McCaw, J. P. Billups, Adolph Proskauer, Julius Weber, J. C. Bush.

Bank of Mobile. Northwest corner Royal and St. Francis streets. Capital $375,000. Wm H. Pratt, President; R. F. Manly, Cashier, Colin K. Humphries, Bookkeeper; R. C. Fodick, Exchange Clerk; L. W. Hammond, Runner. Directors. T. G. Bush, A. C. Danner, Julian W. Whiting, John Maguire, I. C. DuBose, R. W. Hallett, A. M. Quigley. Discount day, Wednesday. Offering, Tuesday.

First National Bank. No. 40 St. Francis. Capital $300,000. J. H. Masson, President; Lloyd Bowers, Cashier; Earle Macartney, Teller; Henry Thompson, General Bookkeeper; Oliver Hagan, Note and Exchange Clerk; Leslie Taylor and Robert Jump, Runners. Directors. Moses Waring, Hy. Hall, C. G. Richards, J. H. Masson, Rittenhouse Moore. Annual election first of January.

Mobile Savings Bank. No. 38 St. Francis street. T. Henry, President; J. Kirkbride, Vice President; J. B. McMillan, Cashier; J. F. S. Wier, Paying and Receiving Teller; John W. Bright, Individual Bookkeeper; J. A. Punch, General Bookkeeper, Note and Exchange Clerk. T. D. McMillan, Runner; A. M. Quigley, Jr., Assistant Runner. Directors. T. Henry, J. Kirkbride, W. J. Hearin, W. H. Leinkauf, M. Macarthy, John Bowen, M. Forchheimer. Discount days, Tuesday and Friday. Offering, Monday and Thursday. Annual election 16th of February.

National Commercial Bank of Mobile. No. 25 St. Francis street. Capital $350,000. Augustus A. Winston, President; A. M. Punch, Cashier; Wm. Demouy, Teller; W. B. Pope, General Bookkeeper; A. J. Hall, Runner. Directors. Charles Hopkins, T. T. Tyree, J. Pollock, Leroy Brewer, A. A. Winston, James McDonnell. Annual election first Thursday in January.

Peoples' Savings Bank. Northeast corner Royal and St. Francis streets. Peter Stark, President; J. W. Whiting, Vice President; L. C. Fry, Cashier; T. S. Bidgood, Jr., Teller; J. W. Little, Book-keeper; John Neff, Runner. Directors. Peter Stark, J. B. Davis, D. T. Parker, George F. Werborn, J. W. Whiting, Albert C. Danner, William J. Brainard. Annual election in July.

Southern Bank of Alabama. Southeast corner St. Francis and St. Joseph streets. Capital $100,000. Henry A. Schroeder, President; Marcellus G. Hudson, Cashier; M. Demouy, Paying Teller; Reuben V. Schuyler, Bookkeeper; Frank D. Nabers, Runner. Directors. D. R. Dunlap, William A. Smith, Thomas St. John, L. M. Wilson, L. Scranton, E. S. Perryman, Wm. L. Baker, Henry A. Schroeder. Discount Day, Tuesday. Offering, Monday. Annual election second Tuesday in January.
Citizens Mutual Fire Insurance. Office 36 St. Francis street. George Byram, President; E. B. Goelet, Secretary. Annual election first Monday before the second Tuesday in July.

organization_name: Factors and Traders' Mutual Fire Insurance
line: Factors and Traders' Mutual Fire Insurance. Office St. Michael street. W. H. Ross, President; R. F. Sparks, Secretary. Annual election second Tuesday in May.

Mobile Board of Underwriters. Office 52 St. Francis street. Wm. H. Ross, President; John H. Higley, Secretary and Treasurer; L. Brewer, L. Touart, John Maguire, Wm. H. Ross, Wm. H. Gardner, George Byram, Wm. J. Hearin. Annual election first Monday in August.
Mobile Fire Department. Office 42 St. Francis street. John Maguire, President; Wm. H. Leinkauf, Vice President; A. DuMont, Secretary. Annual election in April.
Mobile Mutual. Office 24 St. Michael street. W. H. Gardner, President; C. A. Holt, Secretary. Annual election first of July.
Planters and Merchants' Mutual. Office 26 St. Michael street. L. Brewer, President; L. H. Kennerly, Secretary. Annual election in July.
Stonewall. Office 22 St. Michael street. Wm. J. Hearin, President; E. A. Shaffer, Secretary. Annual election the second Monday in May.
Washington Fire and Marine. Office 52 St. Francis street. Louis Touart, President; John H. Higley, Secretary; Annual election in April.
Alabama Gold Life. Office 34 St. Francis street. C. E. Thames, President; Thos. Henry, Sr., Vice President; T. N. Fowler, Secretary. Annual election the first Tuesday in February.
Mobile Life. Office 32 St. Francis street. M. McCarthy, President; J. L. Murphy, Vice President; H. M. Friend, Secretary.
People's Mutual Relief Association.- Office over People's Savings Bank, north-east corner Royal and St. Francis streets.
Medical College of Alabama. Occupies the square bounded north by State, south by St Anthony, east by Lawrence, and west by Cedar streets.

Mobile Medical Society. Meets every Saturday evening at their hall, 106 St. Francis street: E. H. Fournier, M. D., President; W. F. Sawyer, M. D., (Whistler) Vice President; W. H. Sanders, M. D., Secretary; D. E. Smith, M. D., Treasurer. Annual election first Saturday in December.

Mobile Board of Health. Office 21 St. Joseph street: George A. Ketchum, M. D., President; T. S. Scales, M. D., Health Officer; A. H. Sheldon, M. D., Clerk. George A. Ketchum, M. D., five years; E. P. Gaines, M. D., four years; E. H. Fournier, M. D., three years; Wm. H. Anderson, M. D., two years; G. Owen, M. D., one year. Annual election first Saturday in December.

City Dispensary. Located at the Medical College and open all week days from 10 A. M. till 12 m.; open on Sundays from 10 to 11 A. M. Medicines and surgical attendance furnished free to those unable to pay for the same. The attending physicians are: on week days, Rhett Goode, M. D., and W. D. Bizzell, M. D.; on Sundays, T. S. Scales, M. D.; A. H. Sheldon, M. D. Dispensary Clerk.
College of St. Joseph - Spring Hill, six miles from the city, under the direction of the Jesuit Fathers. Very Rev. D. Beaudquin, S. J., President.

Academy of the Visitation - Summerville, Spring Hill avenue, three and a half miles, under the direction of the Nuns of the Visitation B. V. M.; Mother Liguori, Superioress; Sister Mary Stanislaus, Directress.
Cathedral Parish School, for Boys - Northwest corner Dauphin and Franklin streets, under charge of Rev. J. Crowley, S. J., assisted by lay teachers.

St. Joseph's Academy-Jefferson street, between St. Louis and St. Anthony streets, under the direction of Rev. Father P. Im sand, S. J. Teachers in the Female Department, Misses Maggie and Lizzie Driscoll; Superintendent of the Male Department, James Carlin; teachers, Mrs. Maria Wilhelm and Miss Maggie Maguire.

organization_name: St. Mary's Academy and Cathedral Female Parish School
line: St. Mary's Academy and Cathedral Female Parish School -Conti, between Claiborne and Franklin streets. Sister Mary Cornelia, Principal.
St. Patrick's Female Academy -West side Franklin, between Beauregard and Bloodgood streets, conducted by the Sisters of St. Joseph.
St. Patrick's Parochial School - North side of Beauregard, between Claiborne and Franklin streets, under charge of Rev. Edward Kirwan.
St. Vincent's Male Academy -Northwest corner Charleston and Lawrence streets, conducted by the Brothers of the Sacred Hearts; Brother Stephen, Superior.
St. Vincent's Female Academy -Northwest corner of Savannah and Cedar, Sister Barbara, Superior.

Broad Street Church-On the southwest corner of Broad street and Spring Hill avenue. Rev. B. W. Bussey, Pastor. Regular worship on Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7 P. M.; prayer meetings Wednesdays at 7 P. M.; Sunday School at 9 A.M., M. G. Hudson, Superintendent.

St. Francis Street Church-South side of St. Francis, between Claiborne and Franklin streets. Rev. J. O'B. Lowry, Pastor. Regular worship on Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7:45 P. M.; prayer meetings Wednesday at 7:15 P. M.; Sunday School at 9 A. M.; E. A. Shaffer, Superintendent.

Palmetto Street Church-Southwest corner of Dearborn and Palmetto streets. Rev. Z. A. Owens, Pastor. Service on Sunday at 11 A. M. and 7:16 P. M.; prayer meetings Thursday at 7 P. M.; Sunday School at 9:30 A. M.; G. A. Pearce, Superintendent.
St. Louis Street Church-Southeast corner of St. Louis and Dearborn streets. Rev. Alexander Butler, colored, Pastor.
Stone Street Church-On the southwest corner of Chestnut and Cleveland streets. Rev. Benjamin Burke, colored, Pastor.
Third Baptist Church-On St. Anthony, between Conception and Joachim streets. Rev. A. F. Owens, colored, Pastor.
Virginia Street Church-On the east side of Mary's lane, 4 south of Virginia. Rev. Henry J. Europe, colored, Pastor.

Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception-On the west side of Claiborne, between Dauphin and Conti streets. Right Rev. John Quinlan, Bishop; Rev. J. J. Browne, Rev. Fathers Crowley and Keeler, Assistants.
St. Vincent de Paul-On the southeast corner of Lawrence and Palmetto streets. Very Rev. C. T. O'Callaghan, Pastor, and Rev. Jas. McCafferty, Assistant.
St. Joseph's Church-On Jefferson, between St. Louis and St. Anthony streets. Rev. P. Imsand, S. J., Pastor; Rev. J. Serra, S. J., Assistant.
St. Patrick's Church-On Beauregard, between Claiborne and Franklin streets. Rev. Edward Kirwan, Pastor.
St. Joseph's Chapel-Spring Hill. Rev. Father Deynoodt, S. J.
Convent and Chapel of Visitation-On north side of Spring Hill avenue, three and a half miles from the courthouse. Attended by the Jesuit Fathers.
St. Mary's Church-Corner of Lafayette street and Spring Hill Shell road. Rev. A. J. Ryan, Pastor.
St. Francis Xavier Church-On St. Stephen's road. Rev. A. J. Ryan, Pastor.
St. Bridget's Church-Whistier. Rev. Pastor.
Christ Church-On the northwest corner of Church and St. Emanuel streets. Rev. Alexander I. Drysdale, Rector.
Trinity Church-On the northeast corner of St. Anthony and Jackson streets. Rev. J. A. Massey, D. D., Rector.
St. John's Church-On the southeast corner of Dearborn and Monroe streets. Rev. T. J. Beard, Rector. Sittings free. Church supported by the Offertory.
St. Paul's Church-Spring Hill. Rev. F. W. Damus, Rector.
St. Paul's Church-Whistler. Rev. Asa J. Roberts, officiating.

organization_name: North side of St. Francis, between Warren and Dearborn streets. German Lutheran Church
line: North side of St. Francis, between Warren and Dearborn streets. German Lutheran Church.
Shangarai Shamayim-On the east side of Jackson, between St. Louis and St. Michael streets. Rev. Dr. A. Moses, Rabbi.
Franklin Street Church-Southwest corner Franklin and St. Michael streets. Rev. H. D. Moore, Pastor.
St. Francis Street Church-Southwest corner St. Francis and Joachim streets. Rev. F. R. Hill, Pastor.
St. Paul's Church-Southeast corner of Canal and Jefferson streets. Rev. J. O. Andrew, Pastor.
Good Hope Church-Between Ann and Cuba streets, and Spring Hill avenue and State streets, Rev. E. D. Taylor, colored, Pastor.
State Street Church-North side State, between Lawrence and Cedar streets. Rev. Samuel Wilson, colored, Pastor.
Zion Church-Northwest corner Church and Bayou streets. Rev. Robert S. Evans, colored, Pastor.
Mount Moriah Church-East side of Cedar, near Morgan street. Rev. James Hart, colored, Pastor.
Catholic Female Orphan Asylum.-South-east corner of Conti and Franklin streets, under the immediate control of the Sisters of Charity. Sister Mary Cornelia, Superior.
Catholic Male Orphan Asylum.-West side of Lafayette street, north of Dauphin way, under the immediate control of the Brothers of the Sacred Hearts.
Protestant Orphan Asylum.-South side of Dauphin way, west of Broad, under the control of the Protestant Orphan Asylum Society.
Church Home- Episcopal Orphan Asylum, for Girls-Warren, between Monroe and Eslava. For Boys-west side Wilkinson, head of Palmetto.
City Hospital. North side of St. Anthony, between Jefferson and Broad streets, under the superintendence of the Sisters of Charity; Sister Gabriella, Superintendent; E. H. Fournier, M. D., Physician.

Providence Infirmary. South side of St. Anthony, between Jefferson and Broad streets, under the superintendence of the Sisters of Charity; Sister Theresa, Superintendent; Rhett Goode, M. D., Physician.
United States Marine Hospital. North side St. Anthony, between Bayou and Jefferson streets; John Godfrey, M. D., Physician.

organization_name: Grand Commandery of the State of Alabama
line: Grand Commandery of the State of Alabama-Meets in May, 1879, in Montgomery. Wm. S. Foster of Mobile, G. C.; Daniel Sayre, of Montgomery, G. Rec.

organization_name: Grand Chapter of the State of Alabama
line: Grand Chapter of the State of Alabama, Meets regularly in December of each year, in Montgomery. James H. Joiner, of Talladega, G. H. P.; Daniel Sayre, of Montgomery, G. Sec.

organization_name: Grand Council of the State of Alabama
line: Grand Council of the State of Alabama, Meets regularly in December of each year, in Montgomery. James Milton, of Eufaula, M. I. G. M.; Daniel Sayre, of Montgomery, G. Recorder.

organization_name: M. W. Grand Lodge of Alabama.
line: M. W. Grand Lodge of Alabama.- Meets in Montgomery on the first Monday in December of each year. II. C. Armstrong, Tuskegee, M. W.G.M; H. C. Tompkins, Union Springs, D. G. M.; R. W. Cobb, Helena, G. S. W.; J. B. Bankhead, Lamar county, G. J. W.; W. H. Dingley, Montgomery, G. Treas.; Daniel Sayre, Montgomery, G. Sec.; W. C. Bledsoe, Lafayette, G. Chap.; J. M. Kumphe, Lawrence county, G. Marshal; Jos. W. Adams, Ozark, G. S. D.; S. W. Jones, Marengo county, G. J. D.; James Davidson, Montgomery, G. Tiler.
Mobile Commandery No. 2, K. T.-Meets second and fourth Thursdays in each month, in the third story of Temperance Hall, northeast corner of St. Joseph and St. Michael streets.
Alabama Commandery No. 6, K. T.- Meets regularly on the first and third Fridays in each month, at 7 o'clock, P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Alabama R. A. Chapter No. 66.-Meets regularly on the second and fourth Fridays in each month, at 7 o'clock P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.

Mobile City Court. Hon. Oliver J. Semmes, Judge: John R. Tompkins, Solicitor; James P. Kenny, Clerk. The Court holds three terms, an- nually, for both civil and criminal busi- ness. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July, and continue until the last day of each of said months. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in Febru- ary and June, and the third Monday in November and continue until the business is disposed of.
Probate Court of Mobile County. Hon. Price Williams, Jr., Judge; S. G. Stone, Clerk. Regular terms, the second Monday in each month.

The Port Government. The act of the General Assembly approved February 11th, 1979, incorporating the Port of Mobile and establishing the limits and boundaries of said Port, and providing for the government of the same, went into effect on March 11th, 1979. The officers then elected continued in office until the last session of the General Assembly, when an act, approved December Sth, 1990, continued the same form of government and the same officers for the ensuing two years, comprising the following co missioners, one from each ward, and jointly styled The Police Beard.

Port of Mobile.

The act of the General Assembly, approved February 11th, 1879, incorporating the Port of Mobile and establishing the limits and boundaries of said Port, and providing for the government of the same, went into effect on March 11th, 1879.
The following are the executive officers of the Port of Mobile:

Creole Steam Fire Company No. 1. Instituted 1819. House located on the southwest corner of Dearborn and St Francis streets. William A. Alexander, President; Samuel Pollard, Foreman; Simon Durette, First Assistant; Frank Petite, jr., Second Assistant; Joseph P. Andry. Secretary and Treasurer; Thomas Meyers, Engineer; Thomas Grant, Housekeeper. Annual election first Monday in November.

Neptune Steam Fire Company No. 2. Instituted 1819. House located on the north side of St Louis, fourth west of Water. Alfred W. Torrance, President; Foreman; A. Arata, Second Assistant; John McDonell, Secretary; Walter Frobos, Treasurer; Anthony Koppersmith, Engineer; Charles F. Koppersmith, Housekeeper. Annual election 13th of March.

Annual eléction first Monday in January.

Medical. Medical College of Alabama. Occupies the square bounded north by State, south by St Anthony, east by Lawrence, and west by Cedar streets. Faculty. FA Ross, MD Emeritus Professor of Materia Medica and Therapeutics. EP Gaines, M D Emeritus Professor of Clinical Medicine. George A Ketchum, MD Professor of the Science and Art of Medicine and Clinical Medicine. EH Fournier, MD Professor of Materia Medica and Clinical Medicine. Wm H. Anderson, MD Professor of Physiology. JF Heustis, MD Professor of Surgery. Goronwy Owen, MD Professor Obstetrics and the Diseases of Women and Children. Caleb Toxey, MD Professor of Anatomy. W H. Sanders, MD Professor of Diseases of the Eye. Charles Mohr, Jr. Professor of Chemistry. Rhett Goode, MD Demonstrator of Anatomy. Mobile Medical Society. Meets every Saturday evening at their hall, 106 St Francis street. WH Sanders, MD President; Daniel E Smith, MD Vice President; WT Hamilton, MD Secretary; CC Sherrard, MD Treasurer. Annual election last Saturday in December.

Mobile Board of Health. Office 21 St. Joseph street. George H Ketchum, MD President; EH Fournier, MD Health Officer; WD Prout, Clerk. EH Fournier, MD five years; Wm H Anderson, four years; Goronwy Owen, MD three years; George A Ketchum, MD two years; Edmond P Gaines, MD one year. Annual election last Saturday in December.

Port Dispensary. Located at the Port Hospital, and open at all times under the charge of the Faculty of the Medical College and the Superintendent of the Port Hospital. Medicines and surgical attendance furnished free to those unable to pay for the same. The attending physicians are Rhett Goode, MD and WT Hamilton, MD

Hospitals and Infirmaries. Port Hospital. North side of St. Anthony, between Jefferson and Broad streets, under the charge of the Faculty of the Medical College; W. T. Hamilton, Superintendent. Receives and treats to the extent of 50 patients per day from the city, and 12 from the county.
United States Marine Hospital. North side St. Anthony, between Bayou and Jefferson streets; John Godfrey, U. S. M. H. S., Surgeon in Charge

Providence Infirmary. South side of St. Anthony, between Jefferson and Broad streets, under the superintendence of the Sisters of Charity; Sister M. Clare, Superintendent; Claude H. Mastin, M. D., and Wm. M. Mastin, M. D., Attending Physicians.
First Regiment Alabama State Troops. Organized September 9th, 1875. Headquarters Armory Building, north side Church from Royal to Water street.

line: French Mutual Aid and Benevolent Association. Organized 1870. Meets the first Monday of each month, at No. 12 North Joachim. August Lambert, President; C. Weinacker, Vice President; B. Costa, Secretary; Louis J. Dignat, Treasurer.

organization_name: Hay-Makers; or Oriental Order of Humility

Protestant Orphan Asylum Society. Having control of the Protestant Orphan Asylum, is governed by a Board of Managers, composed of four ladies of each of the following denominations: Baptist, Episcopal, Methodist and Presbyterian; Mrs. Dr. Ketchum, First Directress; Mrs. Duncan Parker, Second Directress; Mrs. Hugh Hopper, Secretary; Mrs. Byram, Treasurer; Mrs. L. L. Ruggles, Matron; Miss M. Barkuloo, Teacher. Annual meeting first Tuesday in December.

St. Patrick's Benevolent Association. An organization formed by Irishmen and their descendants, for charitable purposes. McGovern, Treasurer; S. J. Russell, Secretary; John Hickey and George Fuller, Marshals; J. Brown, Sergeant-at-Arms. Annual election December 12th.

STREET RAILROADS. Bay Shore Railroad and Branches. The main road is a belt road, extending from the Mobile and Ohio railroad depots south via Royal to Charleston, west via Charleston to Chatham, south via Chatham to Magnolia Cemetery and New Jersey, east via New Jersey to Wilkinson, north via Wilkinson to Eslava, cast via Eslava to Conception, north via Conception to Beauregard, and east via Beauregard to the point of starting. Branches extend via Washington avenue to the Fair Grounds, and via Tennessee to Frascati. The Government street and Davis avenue branch extends from the corner of Government and Lafayette east via Government to Royal, north via Royal to State, west via State to Davis avenue and thence out Davis avenue to the Catholic cemetery. A branch also connects Government street via Wilkinson direct to Frascati. A branch also extends from the corner of Eslava and Conception, via Conception, Charleston and Franklin, direct to Frascati. B. Moog, President; William Wilker- son, Foreman.

Dauphin Street Railroad. From Commerce street west to near Lafayette Street. D. T. Parker, President; John C. Chamberlain, Vice President; T. N. Fowler, Secretary and Treas- urer; Wm. F. Bullock, Foreman. Office northwest corner of State and Water streets, up stairs.

organization_name: Second District, Southern Chancery Division of Alabama
line: Second District, Southern Chancery Division of Alabama, (counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington) Hon. John A. Foster, Chancellor; Joseph Hodgson, Register. Court convenes in Mobile for this District, on the fourth Mondays of January and June of each year.

Neil McCarron, Criminal Justice. The court has jurisdiction concurrent with the Circuit of all misdemeanors. The Sheriff serves all the processes of this court. All the fines, forfeitures and costs are paid into the County Treasury. It is the duty of the Solicitor to prosecute all cases arising in this Court.

STREET RAILROADS. City Railroad and Branches. The main road is a belt road, extending from the Mobile and Ohio railroad depots south via Royal to Charleston, west via Charleston to Chatham, south via Chatham to Magnolia Cemetery and New Jersey, east via New Jersey to Wilkinson, north via Wilkinson to Eslava, east via Eslava to Conception, north via Conception to Beauregard, and east via Beauregard to the point of starting. Branches extend via Washington avenue to the Fair Grounds, and via Tennessee to Frascati. The Government street and Davis avenue branch extends from the corner of Government and Lafayette east via Government to Royal, north via Royal to State, west via State to Davis avenue and thence out Davis avenue to St. Stephens road. B. Moog, President; William Wilkerson, Foreman.

Dauphin Street Railroad. From Commerce street west to near Lafayette Street. D. T. Parker, President; John C. Chamberlain, Vice President; T. N. Fowler, Secretary and Treasurer; Wm. F. Bullock, Foreman. Office northwest corner of State and Water streets, upstairs.

Port Dispensary. Located at the Port Hospital, and open at all times. Under the charge of the Faculty of the Medical College and the Superintendent of the Port Hospital. United States Marine Hospital. North side St. Anthony, between Bayou and Jefferson streets. W. H. Goldsborough, U. S. M. H. S., Surgeon in Charge.

United States Circuit Court. Convenes in the United States Court Room, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in June and the fourth Monday in December. Hon. Don A. Pardee, Judge; Paul Strobach, United States Marshal; Richard Strobach, Chief Deputy United States Marshal; Geo. M. Duskin, United States District Attorney; N. W. Trimble, Clerk, Chief Supervisor of Elections and United States Commissioner.

organization_name: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama
line: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama. Convenes in the United States Court Room, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in June and the fourth Monday in December. Hon. Jno. Bruce, Judge; Paul Strobach, United States Marshal; Richard Strobach, Chief Deputy United States Marshal; Geo. M. Duskin, United States District Attorney; Henry S. Skaats, Clerk; John H. Wallace, Register in Bankruptcy.

Supreme Court of Alabama. Hon. Robert C. Brickell, Chief Justice; G. W. Stone and H. M. Sommerville, Associate Justices; John W. A. Sanford, Clerk; J. M. Riggs, Marshal; Henry C. Tompkins, Attorney General; J. W. Sheppard, Reporter. The Supreme Court holds two terms annually at Montgomery, commencing on the first Tuesdays in June and December.

Chancery Court. Second District, Southern Chancery Division of Alabama, (counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington) Hon. John A. Foster, Chancellor; Joseph Hodgson, Register. Court convenes in Mobile for this District, on the fourth Mondays of January and June of each year.
Probate Court of Mobile County. Hon. Price Williams, Jr., Judge; Dick Roper, Clerk. Regular terms, the second Monday in each month.

Circuit Court. Hon. Wm. E. Clarke, Judge; Richard E. Jones, Clerk; R. E. Jones, Jr., Deputy Clerk. This Court has two terms annually, on the seventh Monday after the fourth Monday in March and the tenth Monday after the fourth Monday in September, which continue until the business is disposed of.

Mobile City Court. Hon. Oliver J. Semmes, Judge; Francis B. Clarke, Jr., Solicitor; John F. Powers, Clerk. The Court holds three terms annually, for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July, and continue until the last day of each of said months. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February and June, and the third Monday in November and continue until the business is disposed of.

Recorder's Court. For the punishment of violations of the police ordinances of the Port. Meets every morning, except Sunday, at nine o'clock, in the Recorder's Court Room, southeast corner of Conti and St. Emanuelt streets, upstairs. Hon. Richard B. Owen, Recorder.

Street Railroads. Mobile Street Railway Co. This company, organized in 1887, has control and direction over every line in Mobile, except one. organization_name: David Clopton and H. M. Sommerville
line: Supreme Court of Alabama. Hon. George W. Stone, Chief Justice; David Clopton and H. M. Sommerville, Associate Judges; John W. A. Sanford, Clerk; J. M. Riggs, Marshal; Thos. N. McClellan, Attorney General; J. W. Sheppard, Reporter. The Supreme Court holds two terms annually at Montgomery, commencing on the first Tuesdays in June and December.

organization_name: Second District, Southern Chancery Division of Alabama
line: Chancery Court. Second District, Southern Chancery Division of Alabama, (counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington), Hon. Thomas W. Coleman, Chancellor; Joseph Hodgson, Register. Court convenes in Mobile for this district, on the fourth Mondays in June and January of each year.
Probate Court of Mobile County. Hon. Price Williams, Judge; Francis Kiernan, Clerk. Regular terms, the second Monday in each month.

Circuit Court. Hon, Wm. E. Clarke, Judge; Whiting Ames, Clerk. This Court has two terms annually, on the seventh Monday after the fourth Monday in March, and the eleventh Monday after the fourth Monday in September, which continue for eight weeks.

Mobile City Court. Hon. Oliver J. Semmes, Judge; Leslie B. Sheldon, Solicitor; John F. Powers, Clerk. This Court holds three terms, annually, for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July, and continue until the last day of each of said months. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February and June, and the third Monday in November, and continue until the business is disposed of.

Mayor's Court. For the punishment of violations of the police ordinances of the City of Mobile. Meets every morning, except Sunday, at nine o'clock, in the Mayor's Court Room, southeast corner of Conti and St. Emanuel streets, upstairs. Hon. Richard B. Owen, Mayor.

UNITED STATES OFFICERS. United States Circuit Court-Room, third floor United States Customhouse, Hon Don A. Pardee, Judge; Nathaniel W. Trimble, Clerk; Edwin T. Broadway, Deputy Clerk. United States Collector of Customs Office-Willis G. Clarke, Collector; G. L. Long, Special Deputy Collector and Cashier; L. O. Townsley, Deputy Collector and Clerk; W. L. Scott, Marine Clerk. Day Inspectors-W. H. Holcomb, I. B. Ulmer, James W. Lang, W. LeBaron, Night Inspectors-W. H. H. Minge and J. E. Tompkins. Acting Appraiser-Thomas J. Savage. Janitors-John H. Juggins and John W. Little.

United States Commissioners [With powers of commitment for offenses against United States laws] Henry S. Skaats, Nathaniel W. Trimble, William D. McKinstry, Paul A. Ravesies, M. D. Wicker-sham, F. A. Stoutz and Thomas L. Harrison.
United States District Attorney-office, third floor United States Customhouse. John D. Burnett, Attorney.

United States Marshal-Office, third floor U. S. Customhouse. Wm. W. Allen, United States Marshal for the Southern and Middle Districts of Alabama. Richard Felder, Chief Deputy U. S. Marshal; Robert C. Crawford, W. T. Alford.

organization_name: United States Postoffice-Lower floor U. S. Customhouse
line: United States Postoffice-Lower floor U. S. Customhouse. Leslie E. Brooks, Postmaster; Lee F. Irwin, Assistant Postmaster. Stamp, Money Order and Registered Letter Departments on the St Francis street side.
United States Shipping Commissioner-Office, third floor Customhouse. Van B. Gunnison, Commissioner.
United States Signal Service-Office, southwest corner Royal and St Francis. A. Appleton Pritchard, Sergeant in charge; Oscar W. Ball, assistant.

organization_name: United States Chief Supervisor of Elections-Office
line: United States Chief Supervisor of Elections-Office, third floor U. S. Customhouse. Nathaniel W. Trimble, United States Supervisor.
United States District Court-Room, third floor United States Customhouse. Hon. Harry T. Toulmin, Judge; Richard Jones, Clerk.

United States Engineer's Office-Northwest corner of Dauphin and Royal streets, upstairs. Major A. N. Damrell, United States Engineer, in charge. Major Dammell, U. S. A., aided by the Assistant Engineers, H. Harding, Paul C. Boudousquie and Fred Rettig, in charge of U. S. Government improvements in the western part of Alabama, and the middle and southern parts of Mississippi, from Perdido Bay to Pearl River Bay, on the Gulf of Mexico.
United States Local Inspectors-Office second story U. S. Customhouse. Eugene O'Brien, Boilers, and Samuel Taylor, Hulls.

STATE OFFICERS. ELECTED AUGUST 1886. Governor-Thomas Seay. Secretary of State-Charles C. Langdon. Attorney General-Thomas N. McClellan. Treasurer-F. H. Smith. Auditor-Malcolm C. Burke. Adjutant General- Superintendent of Education--Solomon Palmer.

First Congressional District. The First Congressional District of Alabama is composed of the counties of Choc taw, Clarke, Marengo, Mobile, Monroe and Washington. The Representative is elected biennially. The present Representative from the First Congressional District is Hon. James T. Jones, re-elected in November, 1886.

MOBILE COUNTY OFFICERS. Judge of Probate Court-Hon. Price Williams. Judge of the Circuit Court-Hon. W. E. Clarke. Judge of the City Court-Hon. Oliver J. Semmes. County Solicitor-Leslie B. Sheldon. Clerk of the Circuit Court-Whiting Ames. Clerk of Probate Court-Francis Kiernan. Clerk of City Court-John F. Powers. Register in Chancery-Joseph Hodgson. Sheriff-Dick Roper. Deputy Sheriffs-Wm. H. Sheffield, James Donohue, Jas. Woolf; J. C. Torrey, Ed. Pemberton, J. Morgan Brown and Gordon Collins. Tax Assessor-Starke H. Oliver. Tax Collector-Elisha B. Lott. Coroner-James Flanagan. County Attorney-W. S. Anderson. County Surveyor-A. B. W. Kennedy. County Treasurer-S. G. Stone. County Physician-W. B. Pape, M. D. Superintendent of Poor House-H. Parsons. Jailor-Frank R. Jennett. Public Administrator-Joseph Espalla, Jr. Revenue Commissioners-Orville F. Cawthon, President; A. Pincus, George E. Sage, W. N. Bolton and A. Hartley. Representatives in the General Assembly for Mobile County-Daniel Smith, Senator, Daniel Lay, Erwin Ledyard, T. G. Bush and J. C. Coleman.

FOREIGN CONSULS. Austria and Hungary-Wm. F. Stoutz, Consul, 11 south Royal. Belgium-A. DuMont, Consul, 44 St. Michael. Denmark-Joseph R. Edwards, Vice Consul, 4 south Commerce. France-C. Pillichody, Vice Consul, 118 Government. Germany-E. Holzborn, Consul, 39 N Commerce, upstairs. Great Britain-Wm. Barnewall, Vice Consul, 48 St. Michael. Hayti-Charles M. Bancroft, Vice Consul, Otis' Mills. Italy-S. Festorazzi, Consul, 12 S Royal.
First Regiment Alabama State Troops. Organized September 9th, 1875. Head- quarters at Mobile, Ala., Armory Building, north side of Church street from Royal to Water.
Co. A.-Mobile Cadets-O. J. Semmes, Captain; Cary W. Butt, 1st Lieutenant; R. A. Sadler, 2nd Lieutenant.
Co. B.-Lomax Rifles-Frank P. Davis, Captain; W. E. Richardson, 1st Lieutenant
Co. C.-Butler Rifles, (Greenville, Ala.) -J. B. Kendrick, Captain; E. L. Shine, 1st Lieutenant; G. W. Pryor, 2nd Lieutenant.
Co. D.-Lowndes Rifles, (Fort Deposit, Ala.)-E. L. Graves, Captain; John Tal- madge, 1st Lieutenant; W. C. Weaver, 2nd Lieutenant; Silas Tyson, 3rd Lieutenant.
Co. E. Gulf City Guards- Captain, F. McKeon First Lieutenant Commanding; A. C. Ebeltoft Second Lieutenant.
Co. F.-Mobile Rifle Co. - Murray Wheeler, Captain; R. B. Du Mont, First Lieutenant; A. B. Dure, Second Lieutenant.
Co. G.-Cleveland Greys.-(Georgiana, Ala.) J. J. Stewart Captain, First Lieutenant, Lieutenant. Second
Co. H.-Alexander City Rifles.-Jas. D. Dickson, Captain; Edward B. Rainey, First Lieutenant; Thos. W. Harris, Second Lieutenant.
Co. I. Tuskegee Light Infantry-G. A. Hornady, Captain; R. F. Ligon, jr. First Lieutenant; N. S. Grahan, Second Lieutenant.
Co. K.-Conecuh Guards, Evergreen, Ala. M. A. Gantt, Captain; First Lieutenant; second Lieutenant.
Battery A.-Alabama State Artillery- R. H. Scales, Captain; Rudolph Benz, Senior 1st Lieutenant; C. A. Harris, Junior 1st Lieutenant; Joseph A. Marques, 2d Lieutenant.
Troop B.-Baldwin Mounted Rifles.- N. L. Durant, Captain; M. C. Dolive, 1st Lieutenant; N. W. Dolive, 2d Lieutenant.
Associations and Societies. The Mystics. C. DeR. S.-Organized 1831. Annual parade the night of December 31st.
S. I. S. Organized 1842. Annual pa- rade the night of December 31st.
O. O. M.-Organized 1868. Annual pa- rade Mardi Gras night.
I. M.-Organized 1869. Annual pa- rade Mardi Gras night.
K. O. R.-Reorganized 1874. Annual pa- rade Mardi Gras day.
C. C. B.-Organized 1884. Annual pa- rade Mardi Gras day.

Acme Social Club. Organized January, 1888. Richard S. Fuller, President; Charles H. Delhomme, Vice-President; Wm P. Bond, Secretary; Robt. Browning, Treasurer. Floor Man- agers-Jno. A. Kern and Gus. Rayford. Reception Committee-Jno. J. Burns and Walter Drago.

organization_name: A. L. of H. Dauphin Council
line: A. L. of H. Dauphin Council, No. 895-Meets the second and fourth Mondays of each month at K. of H. hall, south side of St. Michael, between Royal and Water. H. P. Watson, Commander; John E. Kane, Vice Commander; A. Grotz, jr., Orator; T. C. McBryde, Secretary; S. Richard, Treasurer; E. L. Cahall, Col- lector; E. R. Quattlebaum, Chaplain; F. S. Frederic, Guide; F. G. Bolling, Guardian.

Athelstan Club. Organized 1875. Rooms northeast cor- ner of Dauphin and St Joseph, Athelstan Building, second and third floor. An- nual election first Saturday in October. Julian W. Whiting, President; David

Baymen's Benevolent Association. Organized 1851. Meets the first Thursday of each month, at their hall, es St. Emanuel 3 s Conti. Annual election first Thursday in April. Gregor McGregor, President; P. C. Hanlon, Vice President; John Barbich, Treasurer; S. E. Thompson, Secretary; G. W. Murphy, Marshal; William Fleming and William Marshall, Doorkeepers.

organization_name: Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners of America
line: Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners of America. Union No. 89. Instituted 1886. Annual election second Sunday in December. Thomas H. Rutherford, President; David French, Vice President; V.J. O'Connor, Recording Secretary; Ed. Marshall, Financial Secretary; Ed. C. Dyas, Treasurer; Chris Bailey, Conductor; James Madden, Warden; Fred Pickhard, Trustee; Joseph T. Demouy, Preceptor.

Can't-Get-Away-Club. Officers--Price Williams, President; James Flanagan, First Vice President; Herbert F. Acker, Second Vice President; Chas. Geisinger, Recording and Financial Secretary; Wm. A. Alexander, Corresponding Secretary; Mosely F. Tucker, Treasurer. Executive Committee-James M. Williams, Daniel Geary, Herman Muller, Daniel W. McGaughey, James Prendergast, P. A. Scheuermann, Peter Burke, Joseph Cady. Finance Committee-E. Carre, M. McInnis, Chas. Werborn, Geo. T. Lyndall, Jno. F. Powers. Members-Alex. Anderson, E. P. Herpin, E. Carre, R. Sheridan, Augustus G. Moore, George R. Dunham, Richard F. Manly, E. Scheuermann, Wm. V. Beroujon. Annual election first Monday in July.

Catholic Female Charitable Society. Meets the first Tuesday in every month at the Female Orphan Asylum, southwest corner Claiborne and Conti streets. Mrs. E. S. Colsston, President; Miss McGill, First Vice-President; Mrs. M. Caulfield, Second Vice-President; Miss Stella Macy, Secretary; Mrs. Clara Byrnes, Treasurer. Annual election second Tuesday in February.

Catholic Knights of America. St. John's Branch No. 37. Organized 423 January 29th, 1879. Annual election third Sunday in December. Rev. E. Kirwan, Spiritual Director: E. F. Long, President; S. J. Russell, Vice President; E. A. Carolin, Recording Secretary; Jos. F. Mitchell, Financial Secretary; John O'Donnell, Treasurer; Andrew Byrnes, Sergeant-at-Arms; Thos. Q. Barnes, Sentinel. Trustees-Jas. H. Hayes, Peter F. McKay, Peter Buckley.

St. Vincent De Paul Branch, No. 389- Organized April 29th, 1885. Officers for 1888: Very Rev. C. T. O'Callaghan, Spiritual Director; James G. Terry, President; F. H. McLarney, Vice President; P. Hueston, Recording Secretary; P. J. Hanlon, Financial Secretary; E. G. Draper, Treasurer; John Brown, Sergeant-at-Arms; S. P. Devery, Sentinel. Trustees-John F. Powers, for three years, M. Smith, for two years, J. F. Barter, for one year. Meets on the second and fourth Sundays of each month at St. Vincent's old Church on Charleston between Hamilton and Lawrence streets, at 7.30 o'clock P. M. from October to May, and at 8.00 o'clock P. M. from May to October.

Congregation Shaari Shamayim. L. Frenkel, President; S. L. Hahn, Vice-President; Nat Strauss, Treasurer; A. Baerman, Secretary; Rev. Henry Berkowitz, Minister; Prof. S. Schlessinger, Organist and Leader of Choir. Trustees S. Brown, M. Wollner, L. Eichold, S. Richard, B. Kahn, M. Forchheimer and G. Jacobson. Board of School Commissioners-J. Cohen, F. Forcheimer, S. H. Solomon, S. Brown, S. Hirshberg and Henry Hanaw. Collector and Sexton-S. Markstein.

Creole Social Club. Organized 1856. Meets the first Sunday in each month. Felix Andry, President; Jerome Nicholas, Vice President; J. B. Guison, Secretary; Armand Rosier, Treasurer; Frank Ponquinette, Steward. Annual election first Sunday in October.
Female Benevolent Society. Has charge of the

Fidelia Club. Organized May 1st, 1884. Rooms 123 Dauphin street. H. Hanaw, President; E. Eichold, Vice President; James H. Zelnicker, Secretary; Ralph G. Richard, Treasurer. Directors-B. Kahn, G. Jacobson, J. S. Simon, S. H. Solomon, S. J. Hahn. S. Markstein, Janitor. Annual election in May.

Firemen's Benevolent Association. Organized September, 1884. Meets the second Tuesday of each month at the Hall of Phenix Steam Fire Company No. 6. Francis Kiernan, President; W. H. Sheffield, First Vice President; A. W. Torrans, Second Vice President; H. P. Robertson, Secretary; R. L. Goubil, Treasurer; Thomas F. Lahan, Sergeant-at-arms. Annual election first Tuesday in September.

Forrest Club. Reorganized November 1st, 1886. Annual election first Thursday in November. W. S. Copley, President and Manager; H. G. Kearns, First Vice President; A. B. Dure, Second Vice President; J. P. Ross, Secretary and Treasurer.

organization_name: F. and A. M. Grand Commandery of the State of Alabama
line: F. and A. M. Grand Commandery of the State of Alabama-Meets in May, 1888, at Birmingham; William H. Dingley, Montgomery, Grand Treasurer; Daniel Sayre, Montgomery, Grand Recorder.

organization_name: Grand Royal Arch Chapter of the State of Alabama
line: Grand Royal Arch Chapter of the State of Alabama-Meets regularly in December of each year, in Montgomery. W. W. Screws, Montgomery, Gr. H. P.; Daniel Sayre, Montgomery, Gr. Secretary.

organization_name: Grand Council Royal and Select Masters of the State of Alabama
line: Grand Council Royal and Select Masters of the State of Alabama-Meets regularity in December of each year, in Montgomery. W. W. McCollom, of Montgomery, M. I. G, M.; Daniel Sayre, of Montgomery, G. Recorder.

organization_name: M. W. Grand Lodge of Alabama
line: M. W. Grand Lodge of Alabama-Meets in Montgomery on the first Monday in December of each year. W. G. M.; M. J. Greene, Opelika, D. G. M.; W. H. Dingley, Montgomery, G. Treasurer; Daniel Sayre, Montgomery, G. Secretary.
Mobile Commandery No. 2, K. T.-Meets second and fourth Thursdays in each month, in the Third story of Temperance Hall, northeast corner of St. Joseph and St. Michael.
Alabama Commandery No. 6, К. Т.-Meets regularly on the first and third Friday in each month, at 7 o'clock P. M.; in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Alabama R. A. Chapter No. 66.-Meets regularly on the second and fourth Friday in each month, at 7 o'clock P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Mobile R. A. Chapter No. 21.-Meets on the first and third Thursdays in each month, at 7 o'clock, P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Mobile Lodge No. 40.-Meets each Monday in every month, at 7 o'clock, P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Howard Lodge No. 69.-Meets first and third Tuesdays of each month, at 7 o'clock, P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Bienville Lodge No. 348.-Meets the second and fourth Tuesdays of each month at 7% o'clock, P. M., in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Athelstan Lodge No. 369.-Holds its regular communication on the second Thursday of each month, in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Bowen Lodge No. 241. Located at Whistler, meets on the first Thursday of each Month.

German Relief Association. Organized 1871. Meets the first Sunday of each month, southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson streets. Annual election first Sunday in January. P. A. Scheuermann, President; Sigmund Markstein, Vice President; Charles Brettel, Secretary; A. Grotz, Sr., Treasurer. Trustees-John Dolle, F. Becht, T. A. Beck, Charles Ramm, A. Kling and George Finkbobner..

Gulf City Social Club. Organized October, 1887. Election every three months. Adolph C. Ebeltoft, President; Frank J. McKeon, Vice President; John F. Holley, Secretary; Alonzo J. Chisholm, Treasurer; Jas. H. Rankin, Fred. N. Norden and Wm. McCord, Floor Managers.

organization_name: Hay Makers; or Oriental Order of Humility
line: Hay Makers; or Oriental Order of Humility. Organized during the reign of King Zoak. Thaddeus A. Blackman, Grand Sultan; John F. Summersell, Grand Prophet; William H. Sheffield, Grand Vizier; James G. Terry, Grand Master of Cavalry; Dave Levy, Grand Captain of

I. O. B. B. Beth Zur Lodge No. 84.-Meets every Monday evening at their Hall, southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson streets. H. Hanaw, President; H. J. Simon, Vice-President; B. Kahn, Treasurer; S. Heid-elberger, Secretary; A. Bloch, Monitor; S. Markstein, Warden; R. G. Richard, Guardian.

I. O. G. т. Magnolia Lodge No. 2.-Meets regularly every Thursday evening at their hall, southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson streets, third story. Union Lodge No. 5.-Meets regularly every Tuesday evening at their hall, southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson streets, third story. Rescue Lodge No. 70.-Meets every Tuesday evening at 212 Dauphin street, second story.

organization_name: R. W. Grand Encampment of Alabama
line: L. 0. 0. F. Grand Lodge of Alabama--Will meet at Birmingham, Ala., on the second Tuesday in May, 1888. The officers of the Grand Lodge for 1888 are as follows: H. C. Weaver, of Huntsville, Grand Master; George R. Ward, of Birmingham, Deputy Grand Master; J. B. Anderson, of Uniontown, Grand Warden; W. A. Shields, of Mobile, Grand Secretary; R. H. Isbell, of Talladega, Grand Treasurer; Oliver J. Semmes, of Mobile, and -, Grand Representatives. The R. W. Grand Encampment of Alabama-Will meet on the second Wednesday in May, 1888, at Birmingham, Ala. Mount Ararat Encampment No. 1- Meets on the second and fourth Thursdays of each month, at Odd Fellows Hall, northwest corner of Royal and St Michael streets. Alabama Lodge No. 1-Meets every Tuesday night at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner Royal and St. Michael streets. Union Lodge No. 13-Meets every Monday night at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner Royal and St. Michael streets. Mechanics' Lodge No. 69-Located at Whistler-Meets every second and fourth Tuesday evenings of each month. McInnis Lodge No. 71-Meets regularly every Friday night at Odd Fellows Hall, northwest corner St. Michael and Royal streets. Grand Bay Lodge No. 73-Located at Grand Bay-Meets every Saturday evening. Napoleonville Lodge No. 81-Located at Napoleonville-Meets every Monday evening. Mobile Degree Lodge No. 1-Meets the first Thursday of each month at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner Royal and St. Michael streets. Mobile Rebekah Degree Lodge No. 1-Meets the third Thursday of each month at Odd Fellows' Hall, northwest corner of Royal and St. Michael streets.

Italian Benevolent Society. Instituted April 11th, and incorporated April 26th, 1875. Meets the fourth Sunday in each month, at their hall, 10 and 12 north Joachim. Annual election in April. Sylvester Festorazzi, President; F. Rota, Vice President; Paola Arata, Treasurer; John Ivulich, Secretary. Finance Committee-Angelo Arata, Paul Vuscovich, B. Natale, A. Dabovich, A. Muscat.

Knights of Pythias. Rescue Lodge No 34, meets second, third and fourth Wednesdays in each month at their Castle Hall southeast corner Dauphin and Jackson streets. Julius Goldstein, P. C.; H. P. Hirshfeld, C. C.; W. B. Frohlichstein, V. C.; O. W. Couch, M. of E.; J. T. Hawkins, K. of R. and S.; Norman Clark, Prelate; George C. Winter, O. G. Semi-annual elections at last meeting in June and December.

Ladies' Hebrew Benevolent Society. Instituted in 1850. Quarterly meetings first Sunday in January, April, July and October. Room in the basement of the Jackson Street Synagogue, between St Michael and St Louis streets. Mrs. L. Bernheimer, President; Mrs. B. Schonfeld, Vice-President; Mrs. B. R. Levy, Secretary; Mrs. M. Forchheimer, Treasurer.

Ladies' Relief Association. Organized June, 1881. Annual election second Tuesday in April. Mrs. B. F. Johnson, President; Mrs. M. Carter, Vice President; E. Harney, Secretary; Mrs. F. Parker, Treasurer; Miss Rose Trenier, Stewardess.
Lee Association. Organized 1875. Meets the second Thursday of each month at the Hall of Mechanics' Steam Fire Company No. 7. Annual election second Monday in January.
Manassas Club. Organized 1861. Rooms northwest corner Royal and St Francis streets, up stairs. C. L. Huger, President; Price Williams, Vice-President; R. W. Hallett, Secretary.

May Blossom Social Club. Organized December, 1884. Annual election third Monday in December. F. Natale, President; H. Stiller, Vice President; D. P. Burns, Secretary; R. Benson, Treasurer; P. Watters and Joseph Jones, Floor Managers.

Mobry Goods Salesmen's Association. Organized July 9th, 1885. Semi-annual election July and January. President, James T. Överby; Vice-President, Edward G. Tarleton; Recording Secretary, John Martin; Financial Secretary, E. J. Grove; Treasurer, W. M. Ryan.
Mobile Gun Club. Organized September, 1880. Annual election first Sunday in September. J. Antoine, President; William A. Scott, Vice President; C. A. Sartore, Secretary; George Boltz, Treasurer.

Mobile Typographical Union No. 27. Organized 1853. Meets the first Sunday of each month in the Hall of Hook and Ladder Co. No. 3. Annual election first Sunday in January. James E. Amos, President; Joseph W. Tillinghast, Vice President; George F. Tuttle, Secretary; Eyre Damer, Treasurer; Egbert Humphries, Sergeant-at-Arms; J. J. White, Finance Committee.

organization_name: Ο. Μ. Δ. Grand Lodge of Alabama, K. of H.
line: Ο. Μ. Δ. Grand Lodge of Alabama, K. of H.- meets in Gadsden, Ala., the second Tuesday in April, 1888. W. S. J. Lampkin of Auburn, P. G. D.; James K. Powers of Florence, G. D.; T. S. Plowman of Talledega, G. V. D.; Fleming Law of Union Springs, G. A. D.; T. Gardner Foster of Montgomery, G. Reporter; J. T. Pierce of Warrior, G. Treasurer; E. W. North of Selma, G. C.; B. Adler of Faunsdale, G. G.; W. F. Robinson of Eufaula, G. Guardian; J. M. Clark of Mount Hope, G. Sentinel; S. R. Olliphant. M. D., of Mobile, State Medical Examiner.
Grand Trustees-W. A. Shields of Mobile, W. F. Wilkerson of Prattville and A. P. Young of Selma.
Representatives to the Supreme Lodge-R. C. Hunt of Scottsboro and D. W. McGaughey of Mobile.

organization_name: Alabama Lodge No. 483, K. of H.
line: Alabama Lodge No. 483, K. of H.- Whistler, Ala. - Meets every week in Odd Fellows' Hall.

organization_name: Mutual Lodge No. 501, K. of H.
line: Mutual Lodge No. 501, K. of H.-Meets every first and third Thursday evenings of each month, at the hall, south side St. Michael, between Royal and Water. Dictator, Abraham G. Levy; Vice-Dictator, Thomas C. McBryde; Assistant Dictator, William T. West; Reporter, George E. Estes; Financial Reporter, E. L. Cahall; Treasurer, L. Frenkel; Chaplain, Neander Crane; Guide, A. Baumblatt; Inside Guardian, Ed. Fichet; Sentinel, William Brabner; Trustees, T. F. Roche, R. F. Manly, D. W. McGaughey. Sitting Past Dictator, Benjamin B. Cox.

organization_name: Cornelia Lodge No. 235, K. and L. of H.
line: Cornelia Lodge No. 235, K. and L. of H.-Meets the second and fourth Thursdays of every month at the Hall, south-east corner Dauphin and Jackson.

Protestant Orphan Asylum Society. Having control of the Protestant Orphan Asylum, is governed by a Board of Managers composed of four ladies of each of the following denominations: Baptist, Episcopal, Methodist and Presbyterian. Mrs. Chas. F. Hopkins, First Directress; Mrs. Geo. A. Ketchum, Second Directress; Secretary, Miss Della Roberts, Miss V. Dorman, Treasurer; Mrs. Anna H. Malone, Matron. Annual meeting first Tuesday in December.

S. P. C. A. Organized November 1885. Rufus Dane, President. Vice Presidents-Rev. Ira W. Porter, Dick Roper, H. T. Toulmin and D. C. Anderson; Secretary, C. R. Eastburn; Treasurer, James W. Holmes; Executive Committee, Dan Harris, Julius Lowenthal, William A. Alexander, T. W. Watkins, B. F. Cody, Dan McGaughey, Thomas St. John, William Turner, Daniel. E. Smith and Rabbi H. Berkowitz. F. G. Bromberg, Counsel.
Spanish Benevolent Society. Instituted 1870. Meets the first Sunday of each month. Annual election in April. F. Canizas, President; B. M. Balbontin, Vice President; Jose Agudo, Secretary.

St. Joseph's Temperance Institute. Located at St. Mary's Hall, south side Springhill Shell Road, second west of Lafayette. Organized 1886. A. Demouy, President; W. H. H. Minge, Vice President; George S. Vautrot, Secretary; E. Johnson, Treasurer. Annual election first Sunday in January.

Terpsichorean Dancing Club. Organized February, 1883. Semi-Annual election in January and July. Adolph C. Ebeltoft, President; C. Martin, Vice President; James McDonald, Secretary; Daniel J. Murphy, Treasurer; Thad. R. Keyland and James R. Hunt, Floor Managers.

Twenty-first Alabama Commemorative Association. Organized 1882. Meets at Armory Hall. Annual election April 6th. Thomas T. Dorman, President; John F. Cothran, Vice President; Frank Kiernan, Secretary; Treasurer.

Workingmen's Benevolent Timber Association. Organized March 5th, 1881. Hall 30 Government street. Annual election first Tuesday in February. T. J. Rowell, President; William Taylor, Vice President; E. G. Dreaper, Treasurer; S. E. Thompson, Secretary; William Fleming and James Donivan, Doorkeepers; P. J. Lynch and John King, Marshals.

Young Men's Benevolent Association. Organized June, 1879. Annual election first Wednesday in June. Francis Kiernan, President; John H. Moore, Vice President; J. F. Junger, Treasurer; F. T. Koppersmith, Recording Secretary; Edward Phelan, Sergeant-at-Arms.

Young Men's Christian Association. Organized March, 1879. Free reading tables supplied with the best current literature of the day. Parlor, game and conference rooms 76 Dauphin street, up stairs. Annual election second Tuesday in October. R. O. Harris, President; J. C. Bush, Vice-President; John Gaillard, Treasurer; B. F. Guille, General Secretary. Directors.-T. C. McBryde, Jno. Gaillard, Geo. S. Moore, J. E. Michael, J. H. Locke, F. J. McCoy, Ira W. Porter, N. Crane, R. O. Harris, J. C. Bush, Geo. Stewart, O. Sibley, W. T. West, Dr. E. Dickson, P. J. Hamilton.
Factors' and Traders' Mutual-Office 9 St Michael street; Wm. H. Ross, President; W. A. Buck, Secretary. Annual election second Tuesday in May.
Mobile-Office 25 St Michael street; Erastus S. Perryman. President, F. L. Glover, Secretary. Annual election first of July.
Planters' and Merchants' Mutual-Office 26 St Michael street; A. P. Bush, President; George A. Pearce, Secretary. Annual election in July.

Baymen's Benevolent Association.-Organized 1851. Meets the first Thursday of each month, at their hall, east side St Emanuel three south Conti. Annual election first Thursday in April. Gregor McGregor, President; J. W. Murphy, Vice-President; John Barbich, Treasurer; S. E. Thompson, Secretary; Dominick Lavene, Marshal; Wm. Marshall and John Vinas, Doorkeepers.

Tutwiler; Secretary, J. S. Owen; Treasurer, C. G. Billing; Tiler, H. M. Hoppner; Esquire, D. H. Lay; Inner Guard, R. E. Wilson; Trustees. Francis Kiernan, D. H. Lay and C. A. Harris.

United States Circuit Court. Convenes in the United States Court Room, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May, and the fourth Monday in November. Hon. Don A. Pardee, Judge; Benjamin W. Walker, United States Marshal; Morris D. Wickersham of Mobile, Ala., United States District Attorney; Richard Jones, Clerk; Reuben A. Lewis, Chief Supervisor of Elections and United States Commissioner.

organization_name: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama
line: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama. Regular term convenes in the United States Court Room, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. The special terms of this court for cases in Admiralty and maritime jurisdiction and cases of seizure under the revenue laws or other laws of the United States, are as follows: The first Mondays in November, December, February, March, April, May, July and October of each year. Hon. Harry T. Toulmin, Judge; Benjamin W. Walker, United States Marshal: Morris D. Wickersham, United States District Attorney; Richard Jones, Clerk.

Supreme Court of Alabama. The Supreme Court holds one term annually at Montgomery, commencing on the first Tuesday in November, and continuing to the last day of the next June. Hon. George W. Stone, Chief Justice; David Clopton, Thomas N. McClellan and Thomas W. Coleman, Associate Justices. John W. A. Sanford, Clerk; James M. Riggs, Marshal; William L. Martin, Attorney General; John W. Shepard, Reporter.

Chancery Court. The Fourteenth District, Southwestern Chancery Division of Alabama, is composed of the counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the third Mondays of April and November, in each year. Hon. William H. Tayloe, of Uniontown, Ala, Chancellor. Joseph Hodgson, of Mobile, Register.

Circuit Court. The State is divided into nine judicial circuits. The First Circuit comprises the counties of Choctaw, Washington, Clarke, Monroe, Marengo, Baldwin and Mobile. In Mobile county there are two terms annually, on the seventh Monday after the fourth Monday in March, and the eleventh Monday after the fourth Monday in September, which continue for eight weeks. Hon. William E. Clarke, Judge; Whiting Ames, Clerk.

Mobile City Court. This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land, and in actions for injury to persons or property. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July, and continue until the last day of each of said months. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February and June, and the third Monday in November, and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon. Oliver J. Semmes, Judge; John F. Powers, Clerk.
Probate Court of Mobile County. Regular terms, the second Monday in each month. Hon. Price Williams, jr., Judge; Horace O. Haynie, Clerk.

Mayor's Court. For the punishment of violations of the police ordinances of the City of Mobile. Meets every morning, except Sunday, at nine o'clock, in the Mayor's Court Room, in the Municipal Building, up stairs. Hon. Joseph C. Rich, Mayor.

United States Circuit Court. Convenes in the United States Courtroom, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. Hon. Don A. Pardee, judge; Benjamin W. Walker, United States marshal; Morris D. Wickersham, of Mobile, Ala., United States district attorney; Richard Jones, clerk; Reuben A.
