organization_name: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama
line: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama. Regular term convenes in the United States Court-room, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. The special terms of this court for cases in admiralty and maritime jurisdiction and cases of seizure under the revenue laws or other laws of the United States, are as follows: The first Mondays in November, January, February, March, April, June, July and October of each year. Hon. Harry T. Toulmin, judge; Benjamin W. Walker, United States marshal; Morris D. Wickersham, United States district attorney; Richard Jones, clerk; Readford Jones, deputy clerk.

Supreme Court of Alabama. The Supreme Court holds one term annually at Montgomery, commencing on the first Tuesday in November and continuing to the last day of the next June. Hon. George W. Stone, chief justice; David Clopton, Thomas N. McClellan, Thomas W. Coleman and Richard W. Walker, associate justices; John W. A. Sanford, clerk; Junius M. Riggs, marshal; William L. Martin, attorney general; John W. Shepard, reporter.

Chancery Court. The Thirteenth District, Southwestern Chancery Division of Alabama, is composed of the counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the third Mondays after the fourth Mondays in March and September in each year, Hon. William H. Tayloe, of Uniontown, Ala., Chancellor; Joseph Hodgson, of Mobile, register.

Circuit Court. The State is divided into nine judicial circuits. The First Circuit comprises the counties of Choctaw, Washington, Clarke, Monroe, Marengo, Baldwin and Mobile. In Mobile County there are two terms annually, on the seventh Monday after the fourth Monday in March, and the eleventh Monday after the fourth Monday in September, which continue for eight weeks. Hon. William E. Clarke, judge; Whiting Ames, clerk; Thomas J. Sheridian, Jr., deputy clerk.

Mobile City Court. This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land, and in actions for injury to persons or property. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July, and continue until the last day of each of said months. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February and June, and the third Monday in November, and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon. Oliver J. Semmes, judge; John F. Powers, clerk; Charles K. Holt, deputy clerk.
Probate Court of Mobile County.
Mayor's Court.
The School Board.
Barton Academy.
City Districts.

Jefferson street Academy-Thomas A. Taylor, principal; Misses Sadie Baker, Grace Russell, Jessie V. Redwood, Metta Thompson, Kate L. Dade, Helen V. Townsend, Lillian Lake and Irene Crowley, assistants.
Orange Grove School-Miss Mary B. Prichard, principal; Miss Mary A. McKenzie, assistant.
West Ward School-Lawrence Moore, principal; Mrs. Harriet B. McGill and Miss M. Spaulding, assistants.
Augusta-Street-Misses Ada M. Gates, Irene M. Mettee, Ernestine C. Baarcke, Sarah R. Calhoun and Dodie Black, teachers.

Broad-Street Academy-William A. Caldwell, c, principal; Artemisia Europe, William R. Gleason, Mattie V. Smith, Mary M. Owens, Georgia V. Williams, Theresa E. Page, Lucy A. Carroll, Leana M. Saxon, Ella Favre and Josephine Blackledge, colored, assistants.
Davis Avenue Boys' School-Miss Sarah E. Garner, principal; Miss Kate Parkis and Benton C. Rain, assistants.
Davis Avenue Girls' School-Miss Martha V. Shivers, principal; Miss Anna F. Coyle and Florence E. Wiener, assistants.
Academy of the Visitation-Summerville, Springhill avenue, 316 miles, under the direction of the Nuns of the Visitation, B. V. M.
Cathedral Creole School-No. 54 south Claiborne street, Miss Mary Harrub, principal.
Cathedral Parochial School-Nos. 400 to 410 Dauphin street, up stairs, conducted by the Brothers of the Sacred Heart, Brother Jerome, principal.
College of St Joseph-Springhill, six miles from the city, under the direction of the Jesuit Fathers. Rev. James Lonergan, S. J. President.
St Joseph's Institute-No. 101 north Bayou street, under the direction of Rev. Father Dominicus P. A. Beaudequin, S. J. Teachers, Sisters of Mercy.

Medical College of Alabama. Founded in 1859. The building and grounds occupy the entire square bounded by Cedar, Lawrence, St Anthony and State streets, fronting on St Anthony street. The annual sessions commence in October.
Mobile Board of Health. Office No. 57 St. Joseph street, George A. Ketchum, M.D., president; Samuel R. Olliphant, M.D., vice-president; T. S. Scales, M.D., health officer; D. T. Rogers, clerk.
Annual election by the Mobile County Medical Society the last Saturday in December.

Mobile County Medical Society. Meets every Saturday evening at their hall No. 258 St Francis street, up stairs. Dr. S. R. Olliphant, president; Dr. H. T. Cox, vice-president; Dr. W. H. Sledge, secretary; Dr. D. E. Smith, treasurer; Dr. O. L. Crampton, librarian.
Mobile Savings Bank.
People's Bank.
WM. H. LEINKAUF & SON.
Alabama Building and Loan Association Organized 1885. Authorized capital $200,000. T. G. Outlaw, president; Jno. E. Michael, vice president; J. H. Tindall, secretary.

Eastern Building and Loan Association, of Syracuse, N. Y. Authorized capital $50,000,000. Local branch in Mobile organized 1890. Earle Macartney, president; Henry Mickle, secretary and treasurer; Faith & Ervin, attorneys. Directors—Officers of the Association, E. B. Lott and Theodore H. Cox. Theo. H. Cox & Co., state agents.
Mobile Homestead Association. Organized January 28th, 1889. Authorized capital $250,000. Charles Spies, president; Henry C. MacEwan, vice president.
WM. H. LEINKAUF & SON, BANKERS.

organization_name: Southern Building and Loan Associa- tion
line: Southern Building and Loan Associa- tion, of Huntsville. Local branch in Mobile organized June, 1889. E. B. Vaughan, president; J. L. Taylor, treasurer; Z. M. P. Inge, attorney and acting agent. Board of directors-D. W. Partridge, Gregory L. Smith, G. E. Sage, P. J. Lyons, Dr. H. T. Inge. Depository, First National Bank, Mobile.
Southwestern Building and Loan Asssociation of New Orleans, La. Capital stock, $20,000,000. Local branch in Mobile organized 1891. Frank C. Horton, general agent.
Fire Underwriters Association of Mobile, No. 80 St Michael street. James G. Terry, compact manager.
Mobile Board of Underwriters-Office No. 7 St Michael street. Wm. H. Ross, president; Joseph F. Mitchell, secretary and treasurer.
Factors and Traders Mutual-Office No. 7 St Michael street. Wm. H. Ross, president; Wm. A. Buck, secretary; Joseph F. Mitchell, assistant secretary. Annual election second Tuesday in May.
Planters and Merchants-Office No. 60. St Michael street. L. Brewer, president; John H. Tindall, secretary; Charles R. Garnert, assistant secretary. Annual election in July.
Stonewall-Office No. 56 St Michael street. W. J. Hearin, president, John Gaillard, secretary. Annual election second Monday in May.
MOBILE COTTON EXCHANGE 1891-1892.

MOBILE CHAMBER OF COMMERCE. Organized April, 1884. Rooms No. 55 North Commerce street. Samuel Brown, president; Jasper S. Knight, vice-president; A. Pope St. John, secretary; John E. Michael, treasurer.

MOBILE SHINGLE EXCHANGE. Organized 1890. Rooms No. 78 St. Francis street, up stairs. G. C. Clarke, president; J. F. Cameron, first vice president; C. W. Butt, second vice president and treasurer; John R. Simpson, secretary.
C. G. Richards & Son, J. D. Cameron & Son, Savage & Morris, Stewart & Butt, Mobile Coal Co., Mobile Shingle & infg. Company.
MOBILE FIRE DEPARTMENT ASSOCIATION.

organization_name: Mobile Hook and Ladder Co. No. 1
line: Mobile Hook and Ladder Co. No. 1. Instituted 1839. House 'No. 208 St. Francis street. J. T. Turner, president; T. H. Chamberlain, foreman; J. W. Flinn, first assistant; S. H. Oliver, second assistant; W. Bidgood, secretary; Isaac Brisk, treasurer. Annual election first Monday in March.

Creole Steam Fire Company No. 1. Instituted 1819. House No. 15 North Dearborn street. Armand Rosier, president; Simon Durette, foreman; Adolph Lalande, first assistant; John Chastang, second assistant; August Parker, steward; Frank Pettitte, secretary and treasurer; Thomas Meyers, engineer; Thomas Grant, housekeeper. Annual meeting first Monday in November.

Neptune Steam Fire Company No. 2. Instituted 1819. President, Alfred W. Torrans; foreman, William F. Bullock; first assistant, S. E. Thompson; second assistant, Ed. Morris; secretary, Wm. E. Seifert; treasurer, Walter Frobos. Finance Committee: Alfred W. Torrans, George Matzenger, Herman Rodiger, F. T. Koppersmith and S. E. Thompson.

organization_name: Franklin Hook and Ladder Company No. 3
line: Franklin Hook and Ladder Company No. 3. Instituted 1833. Annual election first Monday in September. George W. Fordney, president; V. J. O'Connor, secretary; E. W. Russell, treasurer.

Torrent Steam Fire Company No. 5. Instituted 1838. House No. 6 north Franklin street. W. H. Sheffield, president; George W. Jones, foreman; Joseph Godard, first assistant; Charles W. McGraw, second assistant; David P. Burns, secretary; Isaac C. Doyle, treasurer; Charles G. Ayers, steward. Annual election first Monday in February.
Phœnix Steam Fire Company No. 6. Instituted 1888. Annual election in March. R. L. Goubil, president, Joseph Davis vice president; James H. Nelson, secretary; treasurer.

Mechanics' Steam Fire Company No. 7. Instituted 1839. House No. 304 Conti street. Annual election 27th of April. S. A. Leonard, president; J. F. Holley, secretary; S. J. Russell, treasurer; H. McHugh, foreman; John Mallon, first assistant; Thomas Price second assistant; W. J. Gunthorpe, Steward; George Marks, Housekeeper. Finance Committee-S. A. Leonard, P. B. Dixon and P. H. O'Hare.

Washington Steam Fire Company No. 8. Instituted 1843. Annual election first Monday in May. C. W. Soost, president; Wm. Hanlon, foreman; C. J. Livingston, first assistant; E. Marschall, second assistant; T. J. Burns, treasurer; George W. Schumacher, secretary and A. Rehm, steward.

The following organization is maintained solely by the Underwriters of Mobile. Underwriters' Salvage Corps. Organized August, 1879. House No. 208 St. Francis street. Annual election second Wednesday in January. Officers for 1892: Daniel McNeill, president; Wm. J. Hearin, vice president; Wm. A. Buck, secretary and treasurer. Executive committee-Murray Wheeler, chairman; W. J. Hearin and John W. Scheible. T. H. Chamberlain, captain; George W. Fordney, housekeeper.

organization_name: F. & A. M. Grand Commandery Knights Templar of Alabama
line: F. & A. M. Grand Commandery Knights Templar of Alabama-The Thirty-second Annual Conclave will be held in the City of Selma, commencing on Wednesday, May 11, A. D., 1892, A: O. 774, at 12 o'clock M. Sir John A. McKinnon, of Selma, R. E. G. Commander; Sir Charles Wheelock, of Birmingham, V. E. D. G. Commander; Sir George S. Obear, of Birmingham; E. G. Generalissimo; Sir William H. Long, of Mobile, E. G. Captain General; Sir Joseph L. Tucker, D. D. of Mobile, E. G. Prelate; Sir Charles Hunn, of Montgomery, E. G. Senior Warden; Sir Robert L. Douglas, of Mobile, E. G. Junior Warden; Sir John L. Rison, of Huntsville, E. G. Treasurer; Sir Edmond R. Hastings, of Montgomery, E. G. Recorder; Sir Fletcher J. Cowart, of Troy, E. G. Standard Bearer; Sir Milton C. Baldridge, of Huntsville, E. G. Sword Bearer; Sir Philip Milhous, of Selma, E. G. Warden; Sir Frank F. Wise, of Selma, E. G. Captain of the Guard. Committee on Correspondence-Sir George F. Moore, of Montgomery.

organization_name: Grand Chapter Royal Arch Masons of Alabama
line: Grand Chapter Royal Arch Masons of Alabama-The next Grand Convention will be held in the city of Montgomery on Monday, December 5, A. D. 1892, A. I. 2413, at 7 o'clock, p. m. M. E. Grand High Priest-James T. Beck, of Camden. D. G. H. P.-John A. McKinnon, of Selma. G. King-Henry C. Wiley, of Troy. Grand Scribe-Andrew J. Nunnelly, of Talladega. G. Treasurer-William H. Dingley, of Montgomery. G. Secretary-Myles J. Greene, of Montgomery. G. Chaplain-Rev. W. C. Cleveland, of Montevallo. G. C. of H.-Joseph Jernigan, of Troy. G. P. S. William J. Rhodes, of Talladega. G. R. A. C.-Edward S. Gatchell, of Selma. G. Master 3d V. - George F. Moore, of Montgomery. G. Master 2d V.-Thomas H. Anderson, of Gadsden. G. Master 1st V.--John H. Bohannan, of McKinley. G. Sentinel-Charles A. Allen, of Montgomery. Committee on Work-H. C. Armstron, J. H. Johnson, William S. Foster. Committee on Foreign Correspondence-Palmer J. Pillans, of Belknap.

Grand Lodge of Alabama-Meets regularly in Montgomery on the first Tuesday in December of each year. M. W. Grand Master-George M. Morerow, of Birmingham. Deputy Grand Master-Francis L. Petteus, of Selma. Grand Senior Warden-George P. Harrison, of Opelika. Grand Junior Warden-James A. Bilbro, of Gadsden. Grand Treasurer-W. H. Dingley, of Montgomery. Grand Secretary-M. J. Greene, of Talladega. Grand Chaplain-William Cary Bledsoe, of Lafayette. Grand Tiler-Chs A. Allen, of Montgomery. Standing Committee on Foreign Correspondence--Palmer J. Pillans, of Belknap, chairman.

Mobile Commandery No. 2, K. T.- Meets first and third Thursdays in each month, in the third story of Temperance Hall, northeast corner St. Joseph and St Michael. Mobile R. A. Chapter No. 21.-Meets on the second and fourth Thursdays in each month, at 7:30 o'clock p. m., in the third story of Temperance Hall. Mobile Lodge No. 40.-Meets each Monday in every month, at 7 o'clock p.m., in the third story of Temperance Hall. Howard Lodge No. 69.-Meets first and third Tuesdays of each month, at 7 o'clock p.m. in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Bienville Lodge No. 348. Meets the second and fourth Tuesdays of each month at 7:30 o'clock p.m. in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Athelstan Lodge No. 369. Holds its regular communications on each Thursday of every month in the third story of Temperance Hall.
Bowen Lodge No. 241.-Located at Whistler, meets on the first Thursday of each month.

Grand Lodge of Alabama-Will meet at Mobile, Ala., on the second Tuesday in May, 1892. The officers of the Grand Lodge for 1892 are as follows: A. H. Turner, of Huntsville, Grand Master; A. G. Bethard, of Decatur, Deputy Grand Master; Daniel H. Lay, of Mobile, Grand Warden; R. E. Jones, of Birmingham, Grand Secretary; E. Erswell, of Birmingham, Grand Treasurer; O. J. Semmes, of Mobile and H. C. Weaver, of Huntsville, Grand Representatives.

organization_name: R. W. Grand Encampment of Alabama
line: The R. W. Grand Encampment of Alabama will meet in Mobile on the second Wednesday in May, 1892.

C. A. Patterson, of Selma, Grand Patriarch; R. H. Turner, of Birmingham, Grand High Priest; D. H. Lay, of Mobile, Grand Senior Warden; R. E. Jones, of Birmingham, Grand Scribe; E. Erswell, of Birmingham, Grand Treasurer; W. T. Hutchens, Grand Senior Warden; G. L. Waller, of Selma, Grand Representative.
Mount Ararat Encampment No. 1.-Meets on the second and fourth Thursdays of each month at Odd Fellows' Temple, northwest corner Royal and St. Michael streets.
Alabama Lodge No. 1.-Meets every Tuesday night at Odd Fellows' Temple, northwest cor Royal and St. Michael streets.
Union Lodge No. 13. Meets every Monday night at Odd Fellows' Temple, northwest cor Royal and St. Michael streets.
Mechanics' Lodge No. 69-Located at Whistler-Meets every second and fourth Tuesday evenings of each month.
McInnis Lodge No. 71-Meets regularly every Friday night at Odd Fellows' Temple, northwest corner of St. Michael and Royal streets.
Grand Bay Lodge No. 73-Located at Grand Bay-Meets every Saturday evening.
Napoleonville Lodge No. 81-Located at Napoleonville-Meets every Monday evening.
Mobile Degree Lodge No. 1-Meets the first Wednesday of each month at Odd Fellows' Temple, northwest corner Royal and St. Michael streets.
Mobile Rebekah Degree Lodge No. 1-Meets the third Wednesday of each month at Odd Fellows' Temple, northwest corner of Royal and St. Michael streets.
Acme Social Club. Meets first Friday in every month at Pixley Hall. Wm. Drago, president; Phil Rayford, vice-president; Wm. Bond, secretary; J. Walter Drago, treasurer.

Alabama State Artillery Veterans' Association. Organized 1889. Annual meeting second Wednesday in March. Leopold Strauss, president; Henry G. Kearns, vice-president; Alfred R. Rayford, secretary and treasurer.

Alhambra Club. Organized 1889. Rooms No. 125 Dauphin street, up stairs. Annual election in November. C. W. Soost, president; John H. Turner, first vice-president; E. J. Trachy, second vice-president; Adolph Danne, treasurer; Thomas J. Burns, financial secretary; Frank H. Stoll, recording secretary. Governing Committee-C. Spatz, John McGovern, C. Schaeffer, C. J. Livingston, William F. Holt.

line: American Brotherhood of Steamboat Captains and Pilots. Mobile Harbor No. 19. Organized April, 1891-W. T. Pettus, captain; John A. Dorgan, first pilot; Syd Dorgan, second pilot; E. S. Barnes, purser; A. C. Scull, purser's clerk; L. J. Keene, captain's clerk; Daniel Convy, W. H. Grey, S. Q. M.; Alfred Olsen, A. J. Durant, P. Q. M.; Victor Lyles, F. D. W.; William O'Neill, S. W.; T. A. Deering, К. В. Brown, C. T. Bartee, trustees.

Matzenger, Receiver; Jos. C. Gould, Guide; W. A. Porter, I. W.; H. C. Beckurs, O. W.; A. G. Levy, Adam Glass and D. H. Lay, Trustees.

Whistler Lodge No. 43—Organized at Whistler, March, 1891.

organization_name: Carpenters and Joiners of North America
line: Carpenters and Joiners of North America. Union No. 89. Meets every Friday night at Union Hall. Officers elected semi-annu- ally. J. F. Demouy, president; T. B. Foster, vice president; David C. French, pre- ceptor; Henry C. Beckurs, recording sec- retary; Edward Marschall, financial secre- tary; Christopher Bailey, treasurer.
Union No. 92, c. Meets the first and third Tuesdays of each month at hall No. 104 north Scott street.

Catholic Female Charitable Society. Annual election second Tuesday in February. Meets the first Tuesday in each month at the Female Orphan Asy- lum, No. 351 Conti street. Miss Sarah McGill, president; Mrs. E. S. Colston, vice president; Miss M. A. McGill, treas- urer; Miss Stella Macy, secretary.

Catholic Knights of America. Alabama State Council-Meets first Monday in March, 1893, at Montgomery. President R. J. Fowler, of Selma; vice president, Peter F. McKay, of Mobile; secretary, Ernest J. Grove, of Mobile; treasurer, Peter Buckley, of Mobile; supreme delegate, T. H. Carr, of Mont- gomery; supreme alternate, William J. Patterson, of Mobile.

St. John's Branch No. 37-Organized January 29th, 1879. Annual election third Sunday in December. President, S. J. Russell; vice-president, James Kill- lion; treasurer, John O'Donnell; recording secretary, E. A. Carolin; financial secre- tary, Joseph F. Mitchell; sergeant-at-arms, Philip Kearney; sentinel, Andrew Burns; trustees, James W. Hayes, Peter F. Mc- Kay, Peter Buckley.

organization_name: St. Vincent's de Paul's Branch No. 389
line: St. Vincent's de Paul's Branch No. 389 -Organized April 29th, 1885. Annu- al meeting fourth Sunday in December. Officers for 1892: Spiritual director, Very Rev. C. T. O'Callaghan; president, George J. Sullivan; vice-president, John H. Moore; recording secretary, Fred W. Kearns; financial secretary, Walter F. Walsh; treasurer, M. J. Vickers; sergeant- at-arms, M. C. K. of A., Branch 418-Rev. Domini- cus P. A. Beaudequin, S. J., spiritual di- rector; W. J. Patterson, president; M. J. Lawler, vice-president; E. J. Grove, ré- cording secretary; C. W. Oberkirch, finan- cial secretary; John T. Condon, treasurer; C. Skally, sentinel.

Catholic Union of Mobile. Organized 1890. Meets the second Sun- day in each month. James H. Hayes, president; Wm. F. Hurley, vice-president; M. A. Tyrrell, secretary; Frank Canizas, treasurer; R. Farrell, sergeant-at-arms.

Central Labor Council, Composed of Knights of Labor, Farmer's Alliance and various trade unions. Organized August, 1889. J. B. Foster, president; F. Glover, vice president; William T. Jones, secretary; Felix Mc- Cosker, treasurer.

Cigar Makers' International Union. Local Union No. 219. Organized Feb- ruary, 1884. Annual election in Decem- ber. John Juzang, president; Andrew Nicholas, vice president; W. T. Jones, fi- nancial and corresponding secretary; Robert A. Instant, recording secretary.

Colored Veterans' Society. Organized May, 1890. Geo. W. Seawell, president; W. S. T. Fearn, first vice pres- ident; C. Williams, second vice president; Lewis Mason, third vice president; James A. Lyon, fourth vice president; Walter Brazil, secretary; T. J. Ellis, correspond- ing secretary; Rev. F. A. Clinton, treas- urer.

Commercial Club. Organized April, 1890. Rooms 72 St. Michael. A. S. Benn, president; W. H. Fitzpatrick, first vice president; Henry Fonde, second vice president; Ferd For- cheimer, treasurer; W. T. West, secre- tary.

Congregation Shaari Shamayim. L. Frenkel, president; Nat. Strauss, treasurer; A. Baerman, secretary; Oscar J. Cohen, minister; Prof. S. Schlesinger, organist and leader of choir. Trustees-S. Brown, L. Eichold, S. Richard; B. Kahn, M. Forchheimer and G. Jacobson.
Board of School Commissioners-J. Cohen, F. Forcheimer, S. H. Solomon, S. Brown, S. Hirshberg and Henry Hanaw.

Creole Social Club. APPENDIX. Organized 1856. Meets the first Sunday in each month. Felix Andry, president; James C. Reid, vice president; John B. organization_name: Daughters and Sons of St. Luke
line: Daughters and Sons of St. Luke. Hall No. 158 south Lawrence street. Annual election in June. This society has six councils in Mobile. B. A. Gholson, S. G. T., Margaret Mack, S. G. T., Henry Ogden, S. G. M.

F. and A. M.-Colored. The Sovereign Grand Lodge of Alabama meets regularly in Montgomery in June of each year. M. W. G. M., R. R. Mims, of Mobile; grand treasurer, J. H. Binford, of Birmingham; grand secretary, W. G. Alexander, of Montgomery. Committee on Foreign Correspondence-W. G. Alexander, of Montgomery. The following are subordinates in this city: Olive Branch Lodge No. 1; T. H. Garrett, secretary. St. John's Lodge No. 2; B. W. Holt, secretary. Hiram Lodge No. 3; Joseph Brehon, secretary. Gulf City Lodge No. 14; Wm. Miller, secretary. Cumberland Lodge No. 22; P. W. Beck, secretary. Eureka R. A. Chapter No. 12; R. R. Mims, secretary. Palestine Commandery No. 5; Richard H. Brinkley, E. C; Lodge rooms No. 104 north Royal street, third floor.

Female Benevolent Society. Has charge of the Widows' Row on the north side of Eslava between Warren and Dearborn streets, and extends relief to the sick and needy. Annual meeting in January. Mrs. T. H. Garrett, president; Mrs. Lyman Dorgan, vice president; Mrs. William Stewart, treasurer; Mrs. G. B. Shawhan, secretary. Meets in the Government-street Presbyterian church the first Monday in each month.

Fidelia Club. Organized May 1st, 1884. Rooms 217 and 219 Dauphin, up stairs. H. P. Hirschfield, president; Alexander Bloch, vice president; James H. Zelnicker, secretary; Ralph G. Richard, treasurer. Executive Committee-E. A. Pincus, A. H. Spira, E. H. Frolichstein, A. Proskauer, S. H. Frenkel.

Jones, Marengo Co., president; J. A. Sclater, Choctaw Co., vice president; J. W. Cunningham, Clarke Co., secretary; Chas. Spies, Mobile Co., treasurer; D. Henson, Washington Co., Chaplain; G. E. Fortnightly Cotillion Club. Organized 1891. Miss Ella Whiting, president; Miss Corinne Irwin, vice president and manager; Miss Belle Hopkins, secretary and treasurer.

organization_name: G. A. R. Goldwood Post No. 11

Summersell, grand conductor; George Winter, grand sentinel. Meets whenever there is work to do. Election whenever called by the grand sultan.

United States Circuit Court Convenes in the United States Courtroom, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. Hon. Don A. Pardee, judge; Benjamin W. Walker, United States marshal; Morris D. Wickersham, of Mobile, Ala., United States district attorney; Richard Jones, clerk; Richard Jones, jr., deputy clerk; Reuben A. Lewis, chief supervisor of elections and United States, commissioner.

organization_name: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama
line: United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama. Regular term convenes in the United States Court-room, third story of the Customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. The special terms of this court for cases in admiralty and maritime jurisdiction and cases of seizure under the revenue laws or other laws of the United States, are as follows: The first Mondays in November, January, February, March, April, June, July and October of each year. Hon. Harry T. Toulmin, judge; Benjamin W. Walker, United States marshal; Morris D. Wickersham, United States district attorney; Richard Jones, clerk, Richard Jones, jr., deputy clerk.

Supreme Court of Alabama The Supreme Court of Alabama holds one term annually at Montgomery, Ala., commencing on the first Tuesday in November and continuing to the last day of the next June. Hon George W. Stone, chief justice; Thomas N. McClellan, Thomas W. Coleman, James B. Head and Jonathan Haralson, associate justices; John W. W. A. Sandford, clerk; Junius M. Riggs, marshal; William L. Martin, attorney-general; John W. Shepherd, reporter.

Chancery Court The Thirteenth District, Southwestern Chancery Division of Alabama, is composed of the counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the third Mondays after the fourth Mondays in March and September in each year. Hon. William M. Tayloe, of Uniontown, Ala., chancellor; Joseph Hodgson, of Mobile, registrar.

Circuit Court The State is divided into nine judicial circuits. The First Circuit comprises the counties of Choctaw, Washington and Clarke, Monroe, Marengo, Baldwin and Mobile. In Mobile county there are two terms annually, on the seventh Monday after the fourth Monday in March, and the eleventh Monday after the fourth Monday in September, which continue for eight weeks. Hon. James Taylor Jones, judge; B. F. Jackson, clerk; Geo E. Holt, deputy clerk.

Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land, and in actions for injury to person or property. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July and continue until the last day of each said months. The First Congressional District of Alabama is composed of the counties of Choctaw, Clarke, Marengo, Mobile, Monroe and Washington. The Representative is elected biennially. The present Representative from the First Congressional District is Hon. Richard H. Clarke of Mobile, re-elected in November, 1892.

organization_name: The United States Circuit Court, Southern District of Alabama
line: The United States Circuit Court, Southern District of Alabama, Fifth Judicial Circuit, convenes in the United States courtroom, third story in the customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. Hon. Don. A. Pardee and A. P. McCormick, circuit judges; Richard Jones, clerk; Richard Jones, jr, deputy clerk: Frank Simmons, United States marshal; Morris D. Wickersham, United States district attorney.

organization_name: Enited States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama
line: Enited States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama. Regular terms convene in the United States courtroom, third story of the customhouse, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. The special terms of this court for causes in admiralty and maritime jurisdiction and cases of seizure under the revenue or other laws of the United States are as follows: The first Mondays in January, March, July and October of each year. Hon. Harry T. Toulmin, judge; Frank Simmons, United States marshal; Morris D. Wickersham, United States district attorney; Richard Jones, clerk; Richard Jones, jr., deputy clerk.

Supreme Court of Alabama. The Supreme Court of Alabama holds one term annually at Montgomery, Ala., commencing on the first Tuesday in November and continuing to the last day of the next June. Thomas N. McClellah, chief justice; Jonathan Haralson, John K. Tyson, Henry A. Sharpe and James R. Dowdell, associate justices; Robert F. Ligon, jr., clerk; Junius M. Riggs, marshal and librarian; Phares Coleman, reporter; William W. Harris, private secretary to the chief justice.

Chancery Court. The Thirteenth District, Southwestern Chancery Division of Alabama, is composed of the counties of Mobile, Baldwin and Washington. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the third Monday after the fourth Mondays in March and September of each year. Hon. Thomas H. Smith, of Mobile, chancellor; Charles K. Holt, of Mobile, register.

Circuit Court. The State is divided into thirteen judicial circuits. The first circuit comprises the counties of Choctaw, Washington, Baldwin and Mobile. In Mobile county there are two terms annually, on the seventh Monday after the fourth Monday in March, and the eleventh Monday after the fourth Monday in September, which may continue for eight weeks. Hon. William S. Anderson, judge; B. Frank Jackson, clerk; Sextus H. Smith, deputy clerk.

Mobile City Court. This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land, and in actions for injury to persons or property and suits on injunction and attachment bonds. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in November, April and July, and continue until the last day of each of said months. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February and June, and the third Monday in November, and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon. Oliver J. Semmes, judge; John F. Powers, clerk, William B. Powers, deputy clerk.

Probate Court of Mobile County. This court has jurisdiction in probate of wills; grant of letters testamentary and of administration; settlement of accounts by guardians, executors, etc.; sales of personal and real property belonging to distributees, etc.; adjudging the insane and appointing guardians over them; binding of apprentices; allotment of dower; partition of lands, etc. Regular terms the second Monday in each month. Hon. Price Williams, jr., judge; Horace O. Haynie, clerk.

Recorder's Court. For the punishment of violators of the police ordinances of the city of Mobile. Meets every morning (except Sunday) at ten o'clock in the courtroom at the Central Police Station, upstairs. Hon. R. William Stoutz, recorder.

Mystic Societies of Mobile. Mobile can justly lay claim to the title of "Mother of Mystics," for here it was, in America, that the day Shrove Tuesday was first celebrated by maskers, mummers and parades of floats which embodied history, poetry and tradition, revealing bewildering spectacles of beauty. The majority of the senior organizations have passed away, but the junior societies are proving to be worthy descendants of illustrious sires. The following is the list:
C. De R. S. (Cowbellion de Rakin Society.) Organized 1831. Annual parade New Year's eve. Last parade 1881.
S. I. S.-(Striker's Society.) Organized 1852. Last parade New Year's eve, 1881. This organization still exists and gives a ball on New Year's eve.
T. D. S.-Organized 1844. Annual parade New Year's eve night. Last parade 1881.
O. O. M.-(Order of Myths.) Organized 1868. Parade Mardi Gras night.
I. M. (Infant Mystics.) Organized 1869. Annual parade Mardi Gras night. Last parade 1896.
K. O. R.-(Knights of Revelry.) Reorganized 1874. Annual parade Mardi Gras day. Annual ball Easter Monday night.
K. O. M.-(Krewe of Mahomet.) Organized 1892. Annual ball in January.
M. G. P.-(Mardi Gras Pilgrims.) Organized 1883. Reorganized 1897. Annual parade Mardi Gras day.
C. C. B.-(Comic Cow Boys.) Organized 1884. Annual parade Mardi Gras day.
O. O. D.-(Order of Dragons.) Organized 1889. Annual Ball in February.
S. O. C.-(Sons of Cyreniac.) Organized 1897. Annual parade the Monday night before Mardi Gras.

This court has jurisdiction in probate of wills; grant of letters testamentary and of administration; settlement of accounts by guardians, executors, etc., sales of personal and real property belonging to distributees, etc.; adjudging the insane and appointing guardians over them; binding of apprentices, allotment of dower, partition of lands, etc. Regular terms the second Monday in each month. Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in ac- tions to try titles to land and in action for injury to persons or property and suits or injunctions and attachment bonds.

organization_name: Chancery Court The 13th District, Southwestern Chancery Div of Alabama
line: Chancery Court The 13th District, Southwestern Chancery Div of Alabama, is composed of the counties of Mobile and Baldwin. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the 3d Monday after the 4th Monday in March and September of each year Hon Thomas H Smith, of Mobile, chancellor; Carl Holzborn register

Circuit Court The State is divided into 13 judicial circuits. The 13th circuit comprises the counties of Washington, Baldwin and Mobile. In Mobile county there are two terms annually, on the 4th Monday after the 4th Monday in March, and the 4th Monday in October, which may continue for ten weeks. Hon Samuel B Browne judge, S M Smith clerk, W A Conrad deputy clerk, James N Granade solicitor for Washington and Baldwin counties

Law and Equity Court The term commences the first Monday in October and continues until the last Monday in July of the following year. Hon Saffold Berney judge, S H Smith ex-officio clerk on law side, Carl Holzborn ex-officio register on equity side, Miss Stella Black dep register on equity side, Wm A Conrad dep clerk on law side

Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land and in action for injury to persons or property and suits or injunctions and attachment bonds. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in October, February and June, and continue until the last day of each said month. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February, June and October, and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon Oliver J Semmes judge, J A Crane clerk, EJ Huet dep clerk, Francis O Hoffman official court reporter.
Probate Court of Mobile County This court has jurisdiction in probate of wills, grant of letters of administration, and other related matters.

organization_name: Inferior Criminal Court of Mobile County
line: Inferior Criminal Court of Mobile County This court has final jurisdic- tion in all misdemeanors arising within the county of Mobile, and preliminary ju- risdiction. Hon Julius E ing within the county of Mo- bile The justices of the peace within the city of Mobile and in all precincts within 5 miles of the city of Mobile have been deprived of all criminal jurisdiction Hon Julius E Alford judge, M L Campbell clerk

Recorder's Court For the punishment of violat- ors of the police ordinances of the city of Mobile. Meets every morning (except Sun- day) at nine o'clock in the court room at the Central Police Station, upstairs. Has limited jurisdiction of misde- meanors concurrent with jus. tices of the peace and also preliminary jurisdiction of certain misdemeanors and all felonies within the limits of the city of Mobile Hon N R Clark recorder, James J Barry clerk.

United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama Regular terms convene in the US court room, 3d story of the custom house, in this city, on the first Monday in May and the fourth Monday in November. The special terms of this court for trials of causes in admiralty and Maritime jurisdiction and cases of seizure under the revenue or other laws of the US are the first Monday in each month Hon Harry T Toulmin judge, Gilbert B Deans US marshal, Wm H Armbrecht US dist attorney, Richard Jones clerk, Richard Jones jr dep clerk

Supreme Court of Alabama The Supreme Court of Alabama holds one term annually at Montgomery, Ala, commencing on the 2d Monday in November and continuing to the last day of the next June James R Dowdell chief justice, JJ Mayfield, AD Sayre, John C Anderson, R T Simpson, Thomas C Mc- Clellan and A A Evans assoc justices, Robert C Brickell attorney gerl, Robert F Lig- on jr elk, Julius M Riggs marshal and librarian

Chancery Court The 13th District, Southwest- Div of Ala- bama, is composed of the counties of Mobile and Bald- win. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the 3d Monday after the 4th Monday in March and Septem- ber of each year Hon Thomas H Smith, of Mobil chancellor; Carl Holzbor: register

Circuit Court The State is divided into 1 judicial circuits. The 13th circuit comprises the counties of Washington, Baldwin, and Mobile. In Mobile county there are two terms annually, on the 6th Monday after the 1st Monday in March, and the 6th Monday after the 1st Monday in October, which may continue for ten weeks. Hon Samuel B Browne judge, S H Smith clerk, WA Conrad deputy clk, Frank Stone solicitor for Washington and Baldwin counties

Law and Equity Court The term commences the first Monday in October and continues until the last Monday in July of the following year. Hon Saffold Berney judge, S H Smith ex-officio clerk on law side, Carl Holzborn ex-officio register on equity side, Miss Stella Black dep register on equity side, Wm A Conrad dep clerk on law side

Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land and in action for injury to persons or property and suits or injunctions and attachment bonds. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in October, February and June, and continue until the last day of each said month. The criminal terms commence on the first

United States Circuit Court-

Supreme Court of Alabama The Supreme Court of Alabama holds one term annually at Montgomery, Ala, commenc- ing on the 2d Monday in November and continuing to the last day of the next June, James R Dowdell chief justice, J J Mayfield, A D Sayre, John C Anderson, R T Simpson, Thomas C Мс- Clellan and A A Evans assoc justices, Robert C Brickell attorney genl, Robert F Lig- gon jr clk, Julius M Riggs marshal and librarian

Chancery Court The 13th District, Southwestern Chancery Div of Ala- bama, is composed of the counties of Mobile and Bald- win. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the 3d Monday after the 4th Monday in March and September of each year, Hon Thomas H Smith, of Mobile, chancellor; Carl Holzborn register

Circuit Court The State is divided into 13 judicial circuits. The 13th circuit comprises the coun- ties of Washington, Baldwin, and Mobile. In Mobile coun- ty there are two terms annually, on the 6th Monday after the 1st Monday in March, and the 6th Monday after the 1st Monday in October, which may continue for ten weeks. Hon Samuel B Browne judge, S H Smith clerk, W A Conrad deputy clk, Frank Stone solicitor for Washington and Baldwin counties

Law and Equity Court The term commences the first Monday in October and con- tinues until the last Monday in July of the following year. Hon Saffold Berney judge, S H Smith ex-officio clerk on low sido Carl Holsborn ev. officio register on equity side, Miss Stella Black dep register on equity side, Wm A Conrad dep clerk on law side

Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in al civil cases except in ac- tions to try titles to land and in action for injury to per- sons or property and suits or injunctions and attachment bonds. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in October, Febru- ary and June, and continue until the last day of each said month. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February, June and October, and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon Oliver J Semmes judge, J A Crane clerk, E J Huet, dep clerk, Robt Η Mc- Connell official court report- er

Probate Court of Mobile County This court has jurisdiction in probate of wills, grant of let- ters testamentary and of ad- ministration, settlement of accounts by guardians, ex- ecutors, etc, sales of person- al and real property belong- ing to distributees, etc, ad- judging the insane and ap- pointing guardians over them, or binding apprentices, allotment of dower, partition of lands, etc. Regular terms the 2d Monday in each month, Hon Price Williams jr judge; Price Williams clerk

Recorder's Court for the punishment of violators of the police ordinances of the city of Mobile. Meets every morning (except Sunday), at nine o'clock in the court room at the Central Police Station, upstairs. Has limited jurisdiction of misdemeanors concurrent with justices of the peace and also preliminary jurisdiction of certain misdemeanors and all felonies within the limits of the city of Mobile, FK Hale jr recorder, James J Barry clerk

Chancery Court The 13th District, Southwestern Chancery Div of Alabama, is composed of the counties of Mobile and Baldwin. Court convenes in Mobile for this district on the 1st Monday in September and continues until the last day in December of each year. Hon Thos H Smith of Mobile chancellor, Carl Holzborn register

Circuit Court The State is divided into 13 judicial circuits. The 13th circuit comprises the county of Mobile. In Mobile county there are two terms annually, on the 6th Monday after the 1st Monday in March and the 6th Monday after the 1st Monday in October, which may continue for ten weeks. Hon Samuel B Browne judge, SH Smith clk, WA Conrad dep clk

Law and Equity Court The term commences the first Monday in October and continues until the last day in July of the following year. Hon Saffold Berney judge, S H Smith ex-officio clk on law side, Carl Holzborn ex-officio register on equity side, Miss Stella Black dep register on equity side, Wm A Conrad dep clk on law side

Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land and in action for injury to persons or property and suits or injunctions and attachment bonds. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in October, February and June and continue until the last day of each said month. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February, June and October and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon Oliver J Semmes judge, J A Crane clk, E J Huet dep clk, Robt H McConnell official court reporter

Probate Court of Mobile County This court has jurisdiction in probate of wills, grant of letters testamentary and of administration, settlement of accounts by guardians, executors, etc, sales of personal and real property belonging to distributees, etc., adjudging the insane and appointing guardians over them, or binding apprentices, allotment of dower, partition of lands, etc. Regular terms the 2d Monday in each month, Hon Price Williams jr judge, Price Williams clk

organization_name: Inferior Criminal Court of Mobile County
line: Inferior Criminal Court of Mobile County This court has final jurisdiction in all misdemeanors arising within the county of Mobile, and preliminary jurisdiction in all felonies. Hon Henry Chamberlain judge, F W Herrmann clk

Recorder's Court For the punishment of violators of the police ordinances of the city of Mobile. Meets every morning (except Sunday) at 9 o'clock in the court room at the Central Police Station, upstairs. Has limited jurisdiction of misdemeanors concurrent with justices of the peace and also preliminary jurisdiction of certain misdemeanors and all felonies within the limits of the city of Mobile.

Mobile City Court This court has jurisdiction in all civil cases except in actions to try titles to land and in actions for injury to persons or property and suits or injunctions and attachment bonds. Three terms are held annually for both civil and criminal business. The civil terms commence on the first Mondays in October, February and June and continue until the last day of each said month. The criminal terms commence on the first Mondays in February, June and October and continue until the business is disposed of. Hon Oliver J Semmes judge, J A Crane clerk, E J Huet deputy clerk, Robert H McConnell official court reporter.

Probate Court of Mobile County This court has jurisdiction in probate of wills, grant of letters testamentary and of administration, settlement of accounts by guardians, executors, etc., sales of personal and real property belonging to decedents, etc., adjudging the insane and appointing guardians over them, or binding apprentices, allotment of dower, partition of lands, etc. Regular terms the 2d Monday in each month. Hon Price Williams Jr judge, Price Williams clerk.

organization_name: Inferior Criminal Court of Mobile County
line: Inferior Criminal Court of Mobile County This court has final jurisdiction in all misdemeanors arising within the county of Mobile, and preliminary jurisdiction in all felonies. Hon Henry Chamberlain judge, F W Herrmann clerk.

Recorder's Court For the punishment of violators of the police ordinances of the city of Mobile. Meets each day at 4 pm (except Sunday) in the courtroom at the Central Police Station, upstairs. Has limited jurisdiction of misdemeanors concurrent with justices of the peace and also preliminary jurisdiction of certain misdemeanors and all felonies within the limits of the city of Mobile. D H Edgington recorder, Jas J Barry clerk.
